CHAPTER 681 .
AN ACT to authorize John Finlayson & Co. to keep a boom acrossthe
mouth of Beaver creek, in Floyd county , and to provide fees for
boomage ,etc.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That John Finlayson,George M. Hatcher, Jas.
Butcher, Thomas J. Allen and John D. Mays , and
theirassigns,heirs and survivors, are authorized to
erectand keep a boom in and acrossthe mouth of
Beaver creek, in Floyd county, for the purpose of
catching and securing such loose saw logs as may
come againstsaid boom ; and they are authorized to
charge the owners of the logsfivecents per log for
1- Local Laws ,vol.2.

2 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
catchingand holding the same , and shall have a lien
on the said logsforthe charges; and when itbecomes
necessaryto open said boom ,if any of the owners of
the logsshould not be present and able to take care
of their logs,or being present should failand refuse
to do so,then the owners of the boom are authorized
to raft said loose logs and take careof said raftsas
they may deem best and charge a reasonablecompen-
sationtherefor,and shallhave a like lien on the logs
forraftingand caringfor as isgiven hereinfor catch-
ing; saidliensshallbe enforced by action in any of
the courts of this Commonwealth under the general
laws regulatingliens.
§ 2. Said boom shallbe so constructedas to permit
rafts and flat-boats to pass on the demand of the
owners of such raftsor boats, other persons having
control of said raftsor boats : Provided , That noth-
ing in thissectionshallauthorizethe owners of said
raftsor boats, those having control of the same for
said owners, to open said boom whenever itwould
endangerthe lossesof saidlooselogs then held by
saidboom by floatingthem out of said creek.
•§3 . The owners of said boom shall use reasonable
diligencein holding the same by having attached
thereto bull-wheel chains, wire and grass ropes of
sufficientcapacity of strength to make said boom
reasonablysecure: Provided , however, That the own-
ersand keepers of saidboom shallnot be held respon-
sible for unavoidable accidents .
§ 4. This act shall take effectand be in force from
itspassage .
CHAS . OFFUTT ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives.
Approved April 19, 1886.
By the Governor :
J. R. HINDMAN ,
Speaker of the Senate .
J. PROCTOR KNOTT .
J. A. MCKENZIE , Secretaryof State.

CHAPTER 683 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to incorporatethe Adair
CountyAgriculturalAssociation," approvedApril 28,1884.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealthof Kentucky :
§1. That the jurisdictionof the marshals named in
thesixthsectionof an act,entitled"An act to incor-
poratethe Adair County AgriculturalAssociation,"
approvedApril 28, 1884, shall extend two miles in
everydirectionfrom the grounds of said association.
§2. That forand during the days on which said as-
sociationmay hold itsfairs, no person shall, without
the consent of the board of directors firsthad and ob-
tained,erecton any lot or land, road,streetor alley,
withinthe distance of four hundred yards of the
boundary of the grounds owned or leased by said
association, any booth, stall,shed or table,or station
anystand,wagon , cartor other vehiclefor the saleor
giftof any refreshments, confections, cakes, cider,
fruits, melons or drinks of any kind. Any person
who shall violate the provisions of this act shall be
liableto a fine of not less than five nor more than ten
dollarsforeach separateactof selling,to be recovered
by warrantbefore any magistrateofAdair county .
3. The board of directors of said association shall
havepower,through itsmarshalsand agents,during
thedays of said fairs, to remove any booth, stall,
shed,table,stand, wagon ,cart or other vehicle, on or
atwhich any refreshments, confections, cakes, cider,
fruits,melons or drinks are sold or offeredto be sold,
withinthe distance aforesaidof the grounds of said
association,without the consent of the board of direct-
ors aforesaid firsthad and obtained .
§4. That it shall be unlawful for said associationto
allowor permit to be carriedon, within its inclosed
grounds, any shootinggallery, stand, tableor contriv-
ance of any kind whatever,at which any game may

4 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be played, or any shooting or throwing,or any other
thing may be done for pay ,wherein the amount paid
forthe privilegeof doing the said shooting, throwing
or other things done ,shalldepend upon the skillof
the person so shooting or playing, or who isengaged
at any such game or contrivance.
§5 . That for the violationof the foregoing section
of thisact the said association, upon indictment,may
be finednot lessthan fiftynor more than two hundred
dollars .
§ 6. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 19, 1886.
CHAPTER 684 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheCitizens' Gas-lightCompany of Maysville.
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Wm . B. McDonough , Wm . S. Moores ,
Chas . H. White , Samuel S. Riley and Gilbert S.
Judd , their associatesand successors, be, and they
are hereby, created a body -politicand corporate,
under the name and styleof Citizens' Gas -light Com-
pany ,and are made capable of contractingand being
contracted with ; and by the said name are made
capablein law tosue and be sued,topleadand to be
impleaded, in any court in this Commonwealth or
elsewhere ; and to make and use a common seal, and
to alter the same at pleasure; and also to adopt ,
amend and put in execution necessary by -laws for
the government of the said corporation, not incon-
sistent with this charter nor the Constitution and
laws of this State or of the United States .
§ 2. The capitalstock of said corporationshallcon-
sistof sixty thousand dollars,in shares of one hun-
dred dollars each ; and when the sum of ten thousand

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 5
dollarsshallhave been subscribed, the said company
may organizeby the electionof fivedirectors, and
saiddirectorsshallfrom their number electa presi-
dent,vice-president, secretaryand treasurer; the last
twoofficesmay be held by the same person ; the said
officersand directors to hold their officesfor one year
anduntiltheirsuccessorsare duly electedand quali-
fied; and afterthe firstorganizationthe electionshall
beheld on the firstMonday in July of each year, in
which elections, and upon all questions upon which
thestockholdersshallhave a rightto vote,they shall
be entitled to one vote for each share of stock held
by them respectively; and they may authorize their
votesto be castby proxy . The forms of the certifi-
catesof stock and the manner of their transfer shall
be fixedby the directors.
§3. The businessof said company shallbe to fur-
nishgas and gas-lightto the city of Maysville and
thetown of Chester, and the citizensand inhabitants
thereof, by contract with them , for public and for
privateuse,and forthispurpose shallhave the right
toacquireand hold and to dispose of real and per-
sonalproperty, so far as may be necessary for the
prosecutionof its business, and may take same in
partor in full payment of subscriptionsof stock ;
and forthisbusinessand purpose the company shall
have the right to construct and operate gas-works
withinor adjacentto the limitsof the city of Mays-
ville, and shall have the right to lay down its gas
mainsand gas pipes, and make the necessary excava-
tionsfor so doing, through or under any street, or
alley,or road, or passway , within the city of Mays-
villeand town of Chester,Kentucky .
§4. The company hereby incorporatedmay borrow
money , when needed in its business, not to exceed
twentythousand dollars, which shallbe itsmaximum
amountof indebtedness, and may mortgage itsprop-
ertyto securethe payment thereof, and may issue its
bondstherefor,with sixper centum interestcoupons

6 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
attached,saidinterestto be paid semi-annually, and
make the same payable at such times and placesas
the directors deem most advisable .
§5 . The liabilityof the stockholdersshall extend
to and be limited by the amount of stock held by
them respectively, and theirprivateproperty shallbe
exempt from the corporatedebts.
§ 6. This act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage .
Approved April19,1886.

district; and any white widow or alienresidingthere-
in,who isa tax-payer therein, or who has a child or
children to be educated within the ages of six and
twenty years,shallbe deemed a qualifiedvoter under
this act .
§ 2. That the trustees so elected shall constitute and
be styledthe Board of Trusteesof East PointPublic

porate, with power to sue and be sued, contractand

shall vest in them and their successors in office for-

dered under this act may be collected and enforced as

otherofficersof electionto open a pollfor and against

CHAPTER 692 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheSummer Shade Institute,inMetcalfecounty.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That A. C. Taylor, and his successorsin office,
as principal, and E. Witty , L. P. Williams, J. D.
Swope , T. S. Howard , J. R. Witty , S. H. Jones, Wm .
M. Gill, Ben . Shirley, G. B. Lee, W. P. Nunnally ,
Thos . Dipp , J. M. Shive and J. B. Denham , as a
board of trustees, and their successors, shall be a
body -politicand corporate,by the name and styleof
the Board of Trustees of the Summer Shade Institute ,
and by that name shall have perpetual succession;
may adopt and use a common seal,and alteror change
the same at pleasure: but itshallnot be necessaryfor
said corporationto procure a corporateseal,but may
authenticateitssealby the signatureand sealof the
principal .
§2 . That said board of trustees, with the advice
and consent of the principalof said institution, shall
have power to make allby -laws and regulations neces-
sary forthe management and controlof said institu-
tion .
§ 3. That the board of trustees shall elect one of
their number president of said board , three constitut-
ing a quorum . The other members may be electedto
such officesas are necessaryto the proper transaction
of the business of the board .
§4 . When any vacancies occur in said board of
trusteesthe remaining members shallhave power to
fillsuch vacancies,or they may delegate such power
to one member of the board . This they may do in all
matters of business which may legallycome before
them .
§ 5. The principalshallhave power to appoint such
other professorsand teachersas he may deem neces-
sary to instructthe pupils of said institute, and shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 21
render to said teachers such compensation as they
and the principalshallagree upon .
§ 6. The principalshall have power to regulate the
course of study, and to charge such ratesof tuition
as he shall deem proper. He may also appoint a
board of visitorsand examiners for said institute, who
shall constitutean advisoryboard of officers,subject
to change or removalby theprincipal.
§7 . That the authorityof the principalto hold
students amendable for their moral conduct during
the entireterm forwhich theirnames are registered
ishereby recognized.
§ 8. The principalof said institution, with the con-
currence of the othersmembers of the faculty, and of
the majorityof the board of examination,shallhave
the power to grant diplomas and issue certificatesof
proficiencyin the respectivebranches, signedby the
principaland chairman of the board of examination :
Provided , That no degree shallbe conferredupon any
student who is wanting in business power or good
moral character .
§ 9. For the benefitand further protectionof said
instituteitshall be unlawful for any person to sell
any spirituous, vinousor malt liquorsin the follow-
ing boundary of about two miles around the Summer
Shade Institute: Beginning on the Barren county line
where the Glasgow and Burksville road crossesthe
same ; thence with said line to Nobob creek ; thence
up the same and south fork thereofto the Pikeville
road, includingA. D. Hodges ; thence with said road
to the Tompkinsville and Horse Cave road ; thence
with the Horse Cave road to Mt. Moriah Church ;
thence to the beginning,includingS. R. Vetuzat and
Joel Sartin; and any person who shallsellany such
liquors in said boundary shall, upon conviction, be
finedthe sum of not lessthan twenty-fivedollarsnor
more than one hundred dollars for each offense; and
any person who shallviolatethe provisionsof this
actshallbe prosecutedby any courthaving jurisdic

22 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tionof such cases; but thisactshallnot in any man-
ner invalidateor repealan act approved May 5, 1880 .
§ 10. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April19,1886.

CHAPTER 701 .
AN ACT to regulate the voting of stock in Mayslick and Mill Creek
Turnpike Road .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That each stockholderin the Mayslick and Mill
Creek Turnpike Road shallbe entitled, at the annual
electionfor president and directorsof said road, to
one vote for each share of stock up to five; all over
five and under ten, one vote for two shares of stock ;
all over ten shares , one vote for four shares .
§2 . No stock becoming the property of another
shall entitlethe holder of said stock to a vote, unless
said stock has been transferredupon the books of
the company three months previous to the election.
Stockholdersmay vote in person or by proxy .
§3 . All acts in conflictwith this act are hereby
repealed . This act to be in force from and after its
passage .
Approved April 19,1886.

CHAPTER 723 .
AN ACT for the benefit of W. A. Thompson ,late sheriffof Elliott
county .
WHEREAS , on the twenty second day of June , 1881,
a judgment was rendered in the Franklin circuit
court in favor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
againstW. A. Thompson and others,on his revenue
bond , forthe sum of sixteenhundred and ten dollars,
with interestat the rateof ten per cent.per annum
until paid, and costs of suit; and whereas, on the
thirty-firstday of August , 1882, an execution for the
sum of eleven hundred and forty-eight dollars and
twenty cents, balance due on said judgment , was
issued, which executionwas leviedupon a tractof one
hundred and thirty-seven acres of land in Elliott
county,belonging to saidW. A. Thompson ,which was
soldon the sixteenthday of October,1882,forthe full
amount of said balance, and purchased by the Com
monwealth of Kentucky ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the said W. A. Thompson shallhave the
furthertime of two years from the passage of thisact
to redeem the land sold as recitedin the preamble of
this act . And the Auditor of State is authorized to
acceptthe principalsum due this Commonwealth in
full satisfaction for the claim due for said W. A.
Thompson , and upon paying of said principalsum

CHAPTER 724 .

at Burksville, Cumberland county, and to exempt the same from taxa-
tion .
Be itenacted by theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That said congregation, through its trustees,
R. M. Alexander , R. M. Cheek and F. C. Baker ,
and theirsuccessorsin office,shallbe allowed to hold,
occupy and possess a house of worship ; also other
property , including a residencefor the occupancy or
behoof of the pastor in charge, which property shall

thousand dollars .
§ 2. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .

CHAPTER 737 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof HarrisonEaton,of Barren county,and to
change the line between the counties of Barren and Metcalfe.
WHEREAS , Harrison Eaton ,being an old man , and
formerly livingin Metcalfe county, near the Barren

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 99
line, and has recently moved into Barren county,
about fiftyyards from the line; and whereas, it is
much nearer and more convenient for said Eaton to
exercisecitizenshipin Metcalfe county ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county line between Barren and Met-
calfe countiesbe, and the same is, so changed so as
to include in Metcalfe county the residence of said
Harrison Eaton ; but the other lands of said Eaton
shall be listedin Barren county, and taxes paid on
same , which lands adjoin the Gill place.
§2 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 22, 1886.

as heretofore, of funds derived from an ad valorem

existed on the twenty-eighth of March , 1868. Said

villeDistrictTurnpike Company's debt, afterpay-
ing the necessaryexpenses thereof.

as the Owensboro and Russellville railroad tax .

ARTICLE V.
Collector .
§ 1. The tax herein provided for shallbe collected
by an officerto be known as " collectorof the turn-
pike tax ;" he shall be chosen as follows : During
the month of April of the year 1886, and as often
thereafterin the month of April as may be neces-
sary, the board of directorsof said company shall
advertisein some newspaper published in Russell-
ville,by two weekly insertions, for sealed proposals
for the collection of said tax ; said advertisement
shall statethe probable amount to be collected, the
terms of payment of the same to the company , the
amount of bond required of the collector, and the
number of years forwhich the collectionisto be let;
it shallalso specifythe time to which said bids will
be receivedand the person to whom they shall be
delivered .
§ 2. The person making the lowest and best bid
shall be declared elected collectorof the turnpike
tax, and he shall, within ten days after receiving
noticethereof,execute to the company his bond with
security, to be approved by the board, in the sum of
ten thousand dollars, conditionedupon the faithful

dischargeof hisdutiesascollectoraforesaid. Should
he failwithin that time to execute said bond , the
board may , in their discretion,either grant him not
exceedingfivedays additionaltime, or choose the
next lowest bidder, or advertisefor one week , as
aforesaid, to reopen bids within not less than five
days afterthe publicationof said advertisement. In
the latter case choice shall be made as before . Should
the person so chosen failto give bond as aforesaid,the
next lowestbidder may be chosen ; and should he fail
to give bond , then the sheriffof Logan county shall
be declared the collectorof the turnpike tax, and he
shall, afterexecuting bond as aforesaid, collectthe
same .
§ 3. The term of years for which a collectoristo be
chosen,which shall not exceed four, shallbe fixedby

be stated in the advertisement for bids .
§ 4. All bids shall be sealed and shall be delivered

shall be opened simultaneously, and no bid shallbe
considered thatshallbe offeredafterany of the bids
shall have been examined .

open,or by any artor deviceto read the contentsof,
any bid afteritshallhave been deliveredto the presi-
dent or secretaryof the company as aforesaiduntil
the same shall have been opened in the presence of
the board . The penalty for the violationof this sec-

dollars .
§ 6. The compensation to be allowed for the collec-
tion of said tax shall not exceed six per centum of
the amount collected .

best of his ability, perform the duties of said office,

CHAPTER 739 .
AN ACT to authorizeand empower W. W. Robertson,latejudge of
the Marshall circuitcourt,to sign certainorders made by him as such
judge .
WHEREAS , W. W. Robertson, late judge of the
Marshall circuitcourt,omitted and neglected to sign
certainorders and judgments , made and entered by
him as such judge on June 18, 1880 ; and whereas,his
term of officeas circuitjudge expired in September ,
1880 ; and whereas, said orders and judgments stand
recorded in order book No. 8 of the Marshall circuit
court, from page 123 to 148 inclusive; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That said W. W. Robertson is hereby author-
izedand empowered to signsaidordersand judgments ,
and when so signed by him shall be as valid and bind-
ing,and have the same effectas ifsignedby him while
in officeand during the term of court at which they
were entered,and the same effectas if signed by him
as requiredby law.
§ 2. This act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage .
Approved April22,1886.

CHAPTER 780 .
AN ACT toregulatethe saleof spirituous,vinousand malt liquorsin
Allen county .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shallbe the duty of the judge of the
Allen county court, upon a written petition, signed
by at leastone hundred legalvotersof his county ,to
make an order on his order book ,at the next regular
term of his court after he receivessaid petition,
directingthe sheriff, or other officers,whose duty it
shallbe to hold an election, to open a poll in said
county on December 6, 1886, for the purpose of tak-
ingthesenseof thelegalvotersin saidcounty,upon
the propositionwhether or not spirituous,vinous or
malt liquorsshallbe soldtherein.
§2 . It shallbe the duty of the county court clerk
to give to the sheriff, or other officerwhose duty it
shall be to hold the election mentioned in section 1
of thisact,a certifiedcopy of the order of the judge
of the county court,as itappears on his order book ,
within ten days aftersaid order ismade .
§ 3. It shall be the duty of the sheriff, or other
officerwhose duty it may be to hold the election
mentioned in section 1 of this act, to have the order
of the judge of the county court advertisedby

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 185
printedhand -bills, posted in one or more conspic-
uous placesin each electionprecinctin said county ,
within ten days afterhe receivesthe order of the
county judge,and twenty days beforethe election.
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the sheriff, or other
officerwhose duty it may be to hold the election
mentioned in section1 of this act,to open a poll at
each electionprecinctin said county ; and the elec-
tion officersshallpropound to each voter who may
vote the question, "Are you in favor of the sale of
spirituous, vinous or malt liquorsin this county ?"
and his vote shall be entered for or against, as he
directs. The pollshallbe returned as other pollsin
saidelection,and shallbe examined and compared by
the same officers.
§ 5. If itshallbe found that a majority of the legal
votes cast at the electionprovided for were given
against the sale of spirituous,vinous or malt liquors
in the county ,it shallbe the duty of the examining
board to certify the fact, which certificateshall be
delivered to the clerk of the county court, and by
him safelykept until the next regular term of the
county court, at which term the judge thereof shall
have the same spread on the order book of his court ;
and saidentry of the certificatein the order book , or
a certifiedcopy thereof, shallbe prima facie evidence
in allproceedings under thisact.
§ 6. After the entry of the certificateof the exam-
ining board, as above provided for, in the order book
of the county court,itshallbe unlawful for any per-
son to sellany spirituous,vinous or malt liquorsin
the county of Allen; and any person who shall sell
any such liquors, or mixtures thereof,in saidcounty,
shall,upon conviction,be finedin the sum of not less
than twenty-fivedollarsnor more than one hundred
dollars for each offense .
§ 7. The provisionsof this act shall not apply to
any manufacturer or wholesale dealer who , in good
faith,and in the usualcourseof trade, sellsby the

186 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
wholesale,nor a druggistwho sellsfor medicinal pur-
poses on a prescriptionmade and signed by a regular
practicingphysician; but no physician shall make or
sign any such prescription, except the person for
whom itismade isactuallysickand such liquoris
absolutelyrequiredas a medicine ; and any physician
who makes orsignsany prescriptionforsuch liquors,
except as provided forin thisact,shallbe guiltyof a
violationof this act, and , on conviction, fined fifty
dollars for each offense .
§ 8. The county judge shallnot make the order for
the electionuntil the persons signing the petition
have deposited with him in money an amount suffi-
cientto pay for printingand posting the advertise-
ments as provided for, and the feesof the clerk for
making entrieson the order book and other legalfees.
§ 9. If it shall be found that a majority of the
legalvotes cast at said electionare in favor of the
sale of spirituous, vinous or malt liquors, in the
county , then in that event it shall not be lawful to

Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That allpersons convicted of violationof the
laws or ordinances of the town of Lewisburg , and
adjudged to pay a finein default of payment , shall
be compelled to work on streetsin said town , or
employed at such other work as the policejudge may
prescribe until said fineand costs are paid, at the
rateof one dollarper day ; and the saidpolice judge
may also prescribe what task shall constitute a day's
work .
§ 2. That all fines for violation of the laws of the
State insideof the corporate limitsof said town , as
well as those for violationof the by -laws or ordinances
of saidtown ,shallbe paid into the town treasury for
the benefit of said town .
§ 3. To repeala part of section3 of charter: where-
ever it now reads " to elect town marshal ," that
itmay read, " trusteesappoint marshal at the time
a new board go intoofficeor organize."
§4 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
[Became a law withouttheapprovalof theGovernor,April24,1886.]

the General Statutes W. B. Edwards , of Hart county ,
was appointed and qualifiedand acted as elisorfor
seventy-two days, and was allowed therefor by an
order of the Hart circuit court two hundred and six-

teendollars, and , under the opinion of the Attorney
General, the Auditor failedto pay fifty-seven dollars
of said claim ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts draw his
warrant on the Treasurer of the Commonwealth for
fifty-seven dollarsin favor of W. B. Edwards , and
⚫ that the Treasurer pay same out of any money in the
treasurynot otherwiseappropriated.
§ 2. That this act shall take effect from itspassage .
[Became a law withouttheapprovalof the Governor,April24,1886.]
CHAPTER 802 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof schooldistrictNo. 9,in Nelson county.
WHEREAS , A judgment was rendered by the Nel-
son circuitcourt at its October term, 1886, in favor
of the Bardstown and LouisvilleTurnpike Company
against school districtNo. 9, in said county , for the
sale of the school-house and lot on which it stands ,
to pay a debt of about one hundred dollars, of which
about one-halfisdue the State; therefore,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Commonwealth hereby releases to
school districtNo. 9, in Nelson county, whatever
may be due the State in consequence of a suitlately
decided in the Nelson circuitcourt, in which the
Bardstown and LouisvilleTurnpike Company was
plaintiffand schooldistrictNo. 9 was defendant.
§ 2. This act shall take effectfrom its passage.
[Became a law withouttheapprovalof theGovernor,April 24,1886.]

210 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 806 .
AN ACT to make Big Barren river a lawful fence from Allen county
lineto Bowling Green .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That Big Barren river, from where it crosses
the Allen county line and enters Warren county,
until it reaches Bowling Green , is hereby made and
declareda lawfulfence, as to the persons owning land
adjoiningBig Barren river.
§ 2. That thislaw isnot applicableto any person or
persons except those who own land that adjoins Big
Barren river, or extends acrossboth sidesof Big Bar-
ren river .
§ 3. This act to take effectafterAugust 1, 1886.
Approved April 26,1886.
CHAPTER 807 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Sand Hill and Concord Turnpike Road
Company , of Lewis county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company is hereby incorporatedfor the
purpose of constructinga turnpike road from Sand
Hill to the town of Concord , in Lewis county . The
name and style of said company shallbe the Sand
Hill and Concord Turnpike Road Company ; by that
name shallhave perpetual succession, with power to
contract and be contracted with , sue and be sued , and
allotherpowers incidentto corporationsof thischar-
acter .
§2 . The following persons are hereby appointed
commissioners to open books and receive subscrip-
tionsto the capitalstock of saidcompany ,viz: Jack-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 211
son Norris, B. L. Wells , John M. Freemax , Geo .S.
Doyle and W. S. Jeffers, any three of whom may
act .
§ 3. The capitalstock of said company shall be
dividedinto shares of twenty -fivedollarseach, and
the entirecapital stock of said company shall not
exceed the amount necessary to constructsaid road
and erectionof toll-houses and gatesand other neces.
sarybuildingsand appliances.
§4 . When a sufficientamount of stock has been
subscribedto justifythe beginning the work on said
road,the commissioners, or such of them as may act,
shall,at such time and place as they deem proper,
calla meeting of the stockholdersand hold an elec-
tionfora presidentand four directorsfor one year or
untiltheir successors are elected and qualified.
§5 . The width of the road or the width of that
partcoveredwith metal,and the depth of the broken
metal,the sizethereof, and the grade of said road, as
alsothe elevation, shallbe determined by the direct-
ors,a majorityof whom shallconstitutea quorum to
transact business .
§6 . Said directors shall locate said road on the
most practicableroute for that purpose ; may , with
theirengineers, chain carriersand other hands , enter
upon the lands and highways along said route, and
layoffand locatethe same . Said company may also
go upon the lands and highways along said route,
and lay off and locate the same . Said company
may also go upon the lands or inclosures contig-
uousornearto theirroad to examine any land,quar-
riesof stone ; and if the directors and owner or
owners of said land or other necessary material to
constructsaid road or any part thereof, any bridge
orculvertthereon,failto agree to the value thereof,
thepresident of said company may , in the name of
thecompany , apply to and obtain from the county
courtof the county in which the land or material is
situateda writ of ad quod damnum to assess the

212 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
damages to the land or value of material to be used,
and after a tender of the amount of damages assessed
to the owner ,the company ,with every applianceneces-
ssry in the constructionof said road,enter upon such
lands and make said road .
§ 7. It shallbe the duty of the presidentand di-
rectorsto appoint one of theirnumber treasurer, who
shallexecute bond with approved [security],and shall
be responsiblefor all moneys of the company . He
shall keep an accurate[account] of the receiptsand
expendituresof said company in a book kept forthat
purpose, which shallbe open to the inspectionof any
stockholder at any time .
§ 8. The judge of the Lewis county court shallissue
bonds to the amount of one thousand dollars per mile
foreach mile of said road as soon as the company is
organized,and any one mile of said road under con-
tractto responsibleparties.
§ 9. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved April 26 ,1886 .

CHAPTER 810 .
AN ACT toprevent stock from running at large in magisterialdistricts
Nos .5 and 6,in Todd county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. It shallbe unlawful for any person owning or
having care of any stock, of any kind whatever, to
permit the same to run at largein eitherof the magis-
terialdistricts, Nos . 5 and 6, in Todd county, after
this act takes effect; and any person or persons so
offending shall be liable to such penalties and dam-
ages as are herein prescribedand provided for in this
act .
§2 . That whenever any stock of any kind shallbe
found running at largein eitherof saiddistricts, it
shall be the duty of the sheriffof the county, or his
deputies, or any constableof eitherof said districts,
or any marshal of the same ,when saidstock isfound
or presented to him ,to take and impound or confine
said stock, and provide for their feeding and safe-
keeping untilthe owner or owners of saidstock shall
have redeemed the same by the payment of allfees
and costsof every kind incidentto such taking, feed-
ing and keeping ; and it shallbe the duty of said
officerstaking or having said property to notifythe
owner or owners thereof, if living in either of said
districtsor the county (if known to him ), withinfive
days from the taking thereof; and if stock found
offending against the provisions of this act is not
redeemed within fivedays aftersaid notice, saidoffi-
cers shalladvertise(ten days before the day of sell-
ing) such stock for sale at the voting place where
said stock was taken. The sale shall be proceeded
with in the same manner as sales are conducted under
executions for the recovery of debt . The sale shall
be made on a creditof three months , the purchaser
giving bond with good security, payable to the officer
making the sale, who shallfor sale and advertisement

214 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
receive the same commissions and fees that are allowed
forsalesof similarproperty under execution.
§3 . Whenever any stock of any kind shall be
found upon the premises or trespassingupon the
premises, possessionsor inclosuresof any person ,
within eitherof said districts, whether owner , lessee
or tenant, the owner of said stock shall be liable
for all damages and dues, and for all costsincident
to such damage , recoverablebefore any officerhav-
ing jurisdictionthereofin saiddistrict; and the party
injured shallhave a lien on said stock so trespass-
ing or doing injury for alldamages , costs and fees,
and may also have execution levied upon said stock
and the same sold in satisfaction thereof, notwith-
standing itmay be exempt from sale under execu-
tion. Any person findingany stock upon his prem-
ises or upon premises under his control shall have the
rightto take up and turn the same over to any of the
officers named in this act .
§ 4. For taking up stock running at large or com-
mitting trespass, the officershall be entitledto the
followingfees: For each horse, jack, jennet, mule,
colt, bull, cow , heifer or calf, over six months old,
one dollar; and for each hog ,pig,sheep or other cat-
tle, under six months old, twenty -five cents.
§5 . When the bond hereinbefore mentioned in this
act shall be collected, after allthe costs and expenses
connected with the taking,keeping and saleshall be
paid, the officercollectingthe same shallpay the re-
mainder , if any , of the proceeds of the sale over to
the owner of said stock, and the sheriffor officer,
whoever he may be , shallbe responsible on his official
bond for his acts or shortcomings under this act.
§ 6. That any of the officersmentioned in this act
who shall,on hisown knowledge ,or informationgiven
by others,failto take up such stock as may be found
running at large, failsto perform his duty required
by thisact,shallbe subjectto a fineof ten dollarsfor
each offense .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 215
§ 7. This act shall be submitted to the qualified
voters of each of said districtson the firstSaturday
in June ,1886, and itshallbe the duty of the various
officerswho held the electionin August ,1885, at the
voting placesin saiddistricts,to hold saidelections,if
they can be had , and if not, then those that can be
are to appoint in place of the ones not to be had ,
whose duty it shallbe to open a poll at each voting
place in saidprecinctsand propound toeach voterwho
may offerto vote at said election the question : "Are
you fororagainstthestocklaw ?" and recordhisvote
in accordance with his responseto saidquestion. The
vote shallbe certifiedand returned and compared as
requiredinelectionsforStateand county officers; and
in each of said districts,when it shallappear by the
certificateof the examining board that a majority
of those voting in said districtvoted in favor of said
law , then this act shall take effectand be in force in
saiddistrict; and when itshall appear that a major-
ityvoted againstit,itshallnot apply. It shallbe the
duty of the examining board to make out a certificate,
under oath , of the number of votes cast at said elec-
tionunder the provisionsof thisact,giving the num-
ber castat each voting place in each districtfor and
againstthe stock law , and [deliver] the same to the
county judge of said county,who shallcause the same
to be entered of record by the county court clerk.
Said districtsare to pay allthe costsand expenses of
said election .
§ 8. It shall be the duty of the sheriffof Todd
county, at leastthirtydays before said election, to
advertiseby printed notices, stuck up at three or
more public places in each of said districts, that a
vote will be taken on the firstSaturday in June ,
1886, at the various voting places in said districts
for and againstsaid stock law.
§ 9. This act,so far as taking the vote as provided
herein, shall take effectand be in force from and after
itspassage . Approved April 26,1886.

CHAPTER 813 .
AN ACT to amend the act,entitled"An act to incorporate the town
of Williamstown , in Grant county," and the several amendments
thereto .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That sections5, 6, 7 and 9 of an act, entitled
" An act to amend the severalacts incorporatingthe
town of Williamstown , in Grant county," approved
January 31, 1878, be, and the same are hereby, re-
pealed, and the officeof chairman of the board of

CHAPTER 814 .
AN ACT to re-enactand amend section3,of an act,approved March
14, 1872,entitled"An act to amend the charterof Bryantsville, and
furtheramend an act approved February 14, 1871."
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section3 of an act, approved March 14,
1872, entitled"An act to amend the charter of Bry-
antsville,and furtheramend an act approved February
14, 1871," is hereby re-enacted and amended as fol
lows : That the trustees of said town shall have the
power to license hotels, coffee-houses, groceries or
drug stores in said town where spirituous, vinous
or malt liquorsmay be sold, and shallhave the power
to collectfrom any person or persons to whom they
have granted such privilegesan annual tax not ex-
ceeding one hundred dollars, the tax always to be
paid in advance. The trusteesshall have power
to prescriberulesfor the government of such saloons,
hotels, coffee-houses,drug stores, etc., and may , at
any time, for a violationof the rules, suspend such
persons from the right to sell or vend spirituous,
vinous or malt liquors. That the applicant for the
licenseprovided in this sectionshall pay the State
tax and givebond as now requiredby law .
§ 2. That allacts in conflictwith this act are hereby
repealed .
§ 3. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 26, 1886 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 219
CHAPTER 815 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Georgetown and Dry Ridge
Turnpike Company ,inScottcounty.
WHEREAS , The Georgetown and Dry Ridge Turn-
pike was constructed under an act of this General
Assembly , entitled" An act to incorporatea company
to constructa turnpike road from Covington through
Williamstown and Georgetown to Lexington, in Fay-
ette county ," approved February 22, 1834, and under
an act, entitled"An act to amend the charterof the
Covington and Lexington Turnpike Road Company ,
and to incorporatethe Georgetown and Dry Ridge
Turnpike Road Company as a separateand independ-
entcompany ," approved March 22,1851 ; and whereas,
under the general statute law of the State then in
force, the board of internalimprovement had the
power and authorityto supervisethe constructionof
turnpikes in which the State was a stockholder, and
to change the grades, width and manner of construct-
ing said roads from those prescribedby the charters
of said turnpike road companies ; and whereas, the
Georgetown and Dry Ridge Turnpike was constructed
according to the plans and specificationsprescribedby
and under the supervisionof the board of internal
improvement , which road, when completed, was ac-
cepted and receivedby the Stateand by said turnpike
company as thus constructed,and has been in use for
more than thirtyyears ; and whereas,the plan, speci-
ficationsand rules so prescribedby the board of in-
ternal improvement under which said road was built
and accepted have been lostor destroyed; therefore,
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Georgetown and Dry Ridge Turnpike
Road , as constructedand built from Georgetown to
the Grant county line,be declared to be constructed
according to the provisions of the acts of General

220 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Assembly recitedin the above preamble , as modified
and changed by the State board of internalimprove-
ment ,and from the passage of thisact said turnpike
road company shallbe deemed to have complied with
all the terms and conditions of the laws under which
the same was built and constructed .
§2 . That for all violationsof the laws applicable
to turnpike roads said company shall be proceeded
against in the manner and be subjected to all the
penalties prescribed by the General Statutes.
§3 . None of the provisionsof thisact shallbe con-
strued as releasingsaid turnpike company from lia-
bilityforfailureto keep saidroad in a safeand proper
condition for travel .
§ 4. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 26, 1886 .

CHAPTER 817 .
AN ACT toestablishan additionalmagisterialdistrictin Todd county.

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That there shallbe, and ishereby, established
inTodd county, an additionalmagisterialdistrict,the
same to be a voting precinct, and have all the rights,
powers and privilegesof the other magisterialdis-
trictsin saidcounty,and be known and designatedas
districtnumber eight (8), and bounded as follows:
Beginning in the center of the public road leading

uous placesin said town thirtydays before any such
election .

their said officeof trustee for from one to fiveyears
respectively; that isto say, one shallhold officefor
one year, one for two years, one for three years,
one for four years,and one for fiveyears, and said
trusteesshalldetermine by lot theirrespectiveterms;
and annually hereafterthe qualifiedwhite votersof
said town shall elect one school trustee,which election
shallbe held on the firstSaturday in May by such
officers of election as the town trustees of said town
may designatefor the purpose, consistingof a clerk
and two judges,who shall take an oath to honestly
and faithfullyperform their duty , and who shall,
within threedays afterthe election,certifythe result
to the board of school trustees, and admit as a mem-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 239
ber of theirboard the person receivingthe highest
number of votes,or in case of a tievote between two
or more candidates, said board shall prescribethe
mode of determining the choicebetween them .
§ 2. Said school trusteesshall,beforeenteringupon
the discharge of theirduties,severallytake an oath to
honestly and faithfullydischarge theirduty as such
trustees .
§3 . The board of school trustees shall have exclu-
sivecontrolover the schoolbuildingand grounds,and
itshallbe theirduty to keep them in good condition
and repair; and they shallhave allthe power to levy
and collect taxes for the maintenance of the Hartford
graded school, known as Hartford College; that the
town trusteesunder the actapproved March 16,1878,
entitled"An act to maintain a graded school in the
town of Hartford, and to provide for the erectionof
school buildingstherein," and the acts amendatory
thereof, but in their discretionthey may levy and
collectin any one year, for the purpose of maintain-
ing the school,only so much tax as may be sufficient,
when added to the publicschool fund to which said
school may be entitledunder existinglaw ,to provide
for the teaching of the common school course of
instruction for ten months of the year ; and said trus-
tees may prescribea rate of tuitionto be paid by
patrons whose childrenor wards are taught in studies
above the common school course of instruction, and
may enforcethe payment of such tuitionby suit; and
in theeventof failureorrefusalof any patrontopay
such tuition,the board may suspend thepupilon whose
account such tuition is demanded from the classes in
which such higher studies are taught .
§4 . The board may fillby appointment , tillthe
next regular election, any vacancy occurringin the
board by death, resignation, removal or failureto
qualify .
§5 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 26,1886.

CHAPTER 840 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe London,Manchesterand Whitesburg Tele-
phone and Telegraph Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That H. C. Eversole and W. R. Ramsey , of
Laurel county ; C. B. Lyttle,W. J. Caudilland B. P.
White ,of Clay county ; JesseBoggs and John Lewis ,
of Lesliecounty ; J. C. Eversoleand John Campbell ,
of Perry county ; W. G. Caudill and W. H. Nickles ,
of Letcher county, and their associates, successors
and assigns, be, and they are hereby, created a body-
corporateand politic,under the name and styleof the
" London ,Manchester and Whitesburg Telephone and
Telegraph Company ," with power under said name to
contractand be contractedwith,sue and be sued , to
have perpetual successionand a common seal, and
such otherpowers as hereingranted.
§ 2. The saidcompany shallhave power and author-
ity to construct, maintain, equip and operate a tele-
phone or telegraph line, or both, from London , in
Laurel county, to Manchester, in Clay county ;to
Hyden ,in Lesliecounty; to Whitesburg, in Letcher
county ;to Woodbine , in Whitley county ; and such
branch lines to and from said points to other points
in said countiesor adjoiningcountiesas the company
may deem necessary .
§ 3. Said company may acquire rightsof way for
their main or branch lines in the same manner as now
provided by the law of this Commonwealth for the
acquisitionof rights of way by turnpike road com-
panies .
§ 4. Said company may fix the rate of charges for
the transmission of messages over their lines.
§ 5. The capitalstock of saidcompany shallbe five
thousand dollars, to be divided into shares of ten
dollars each .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 241
§6 . The businessof saidcompany shallbe managed
by a board of fivedirectors,to be chosen annually by
a vote of the stockholders, at which electioneach
share shall be entitled to a vote .
§ 7. The board of directors shall choose one of their
number president, whose duties shall be those inci-
dent to that officeand provided by said board . They
shallhave power to appoint a secretaryand treasurer,
and such other agents as may be deemed necessary
for the interestsof the business, and may pay them
such compensation as they may deem reasonable; and
saidcompany may acquireand hold allpropertyneces-
sary to enable them to carry out the purposes and
provisions of this act.
§8 . Said company ,by itsboard of directors, may
make allby -laws, rules and regulationsto carry on
its affairs, not inconsistent with the Constitution and
laws of this State or of the United States .
§ 9. Said company may organizeand begin the con-
struction of said main lines when two hundred and
fiftydollars of the capital stock shall have been sub-
scribed and paid in. Each of said incorporatorsare
authorized to solicitand take subscriptionsof stock
to said company . When two hundred and fiftydol-
larsshallhave been subscribed,then the incorporators
shall call a meeting of the stockholders, at which
time a board of directors shall be elected to serve
until the firstregular election,and untiltheirsucces-
sors are electedand qualified.
§ 10. The private property of stockholdersin this
company shallnot be subjectto nor in any way liable
for the debts of the company .
§ 11. No person not a stockholderin said company
shall hold any officetherein,nor shallany stockholder,
who has not paid allcallsmade by the president and
board of directorson stock subscribed or held by
him , vote at any electionor in any matter, and all
16- Local Laws , vol. 2.

242 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
officersappointed by said board of directorsmay be
removed at the pleasure of said board .
§ 12. Two members of the board of directors and
the president, or three without the president, shall
constitutea quorum for the transactionof business.
§ 13. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage .
Approved April 26, 1886.
CHAPTER 842 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Great Western Stock Breeders' Association.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company is hereby created under the

Association, of McLean county, Kentucky , for the
purpose of protectingand perpetuatingof the purity
in high blooded stock of allnames and grades ; and
said company may organize, being now stockholders,

Kentucky , secretary, and E. H. Bland , of Nelson
county,Kentucky ,treasurer; the fivepersons herein
named being the board of directory; the name and
style of the company to be perpetual. An election
may be held annually for the electionof officersof
the company , commencing with the date of the pas-
sage of this act.
§2 . The capitalstock of the company being five
thousand acres of land,valued at twenty dollarsper
acre; may sellbonds of said company not to exceed
seventy-fivethousand dollars,securing theirpayment
under such contractsor mortgage as are granted by
the statutorylaw of Kentucky ; and may increasethe

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 243
company's facilitiesforthe purposes stated in article
1 by purchase of otherlands and otherwise; allbonds ,
deed , mortgages or sharessold by the company shall
be signed by the presidentand countersignedby the
secretaryof saidcompany .
§ 3. The principalofficeof thiscompany shallbe in
the town of Sacramento , and may erect and build on
theirlands such buildings, saw-mills,put in or add
to such machinery, enginesand fixturesas may be
deemed convenient and proper for establishingand
conducting any and all appropriate business under
this actsaidcompany may see proper to theirinterest
to engage in ; that is,within the limitof a strictcon-
structionof the statutorylaws that now exist. The
individualproperty of the stockholdersof this com-
pany shallnot be liableforthe debtsof saidcompany .
§ 4. Said company shallhave the rightto a common
sealupon organizationby charteredauthority; may
make such by -laws forthe government and controlof
their business as they may see proper, and alteror
amend at pleasure,by a majority vote of the stock-
holders,each one hundred dollars' stock entitlingthe
holder to one additionalvote ; may build tramways to
any railroador water course, not exceeding six miles,
for carrying coal, timber, products, manufacture or
supplies; and in buildingsaidtramway , should itrun
through the lands of othersnot belonging to the com-
pany , upon condition such right cannot be bought
for a faircash value,it may be condemned and paid
for as the statutorylaw of Kentucky defines.
§5 . Said company may deed to the purchaser of
shares of stock an interestpro rata with the amount
to the whole as set forth in article 2 .
§ 6. Said partiesas named in article1,theirassoci-
ates,successorsand assigns,be,and they arehereby,
constituteda body -corporateand politic, by the name
and styleGreat Western Stock Breeders' Association,
of McLean county, Kentucky ,with power to contract
and be contracted with ; to own property, both real

244 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and personal, and to sue and be sued in all courts
and places,and to do other acts,and have allother
powers needfulfor the successfulprosecutionof their
business, and for the execution of the powers herein
granted ; and that W. L. Bland aad E. H. Bland are
authorized commissioners to make sale of fiftythou-
sand dollars, in one hundred dollar shares, of com-
pany's real estate. Said company's secretaryshall
keep a book of all accounts,which shall be at any
and alltimes accessibleto the inspectionof all stock-
holders .
§ 7. Said corporationis not asking,nor is thisact
to so be construed, as grantingany privilegesin viola-
tionof the Constitutionand laws of Kentucky or of
the United States .
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April26,1886.

said three or more directors when five hundred shares

directors .

business .

§ 4. That said bank may transact business as a natu-
ral person ; may loan money , discount promissory

ers ; officersand members of the board of directors

and consolidatewith any other associationnow exist-
ing or hereafter formed in the city of Lexington,
unitingwith any such associationso desiringto unite,
under the name of this association or of that associa-
tion so united with ; and the consolidated associa-
tionshallhave and exerciseallthe rights, powers and
privilegesheld and enjoyed by eitheror both of the
associations so consolidated .
§3 . This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage.

CHAPTER 862 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the American Mutual Aid Society.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That said societyis hereby authorized to issue
certificatesof endowment , by which the said society
shallpay to itsmembers designated sums of money
upon their reaching certain agreed ages. The said
society, in computing the annual payments for such
endowment ,shalluse the same tableof mortalityas
isused by itfor fixingitschargesforpaying death
losses. To such premium the board of directorsor its
executive committee may add such percentage for
costof collectionand management as may be deemed
fairand necessary. The premiums so receivedfrom
time to time shallbe invested only in such securities
as are provided forby the insurancelaws of thisCom-
monwealth . The societyshallnot issuean endowment
certificateto any one whose lifeis not insured in said
society, nor shall it issuea certificateforany greater
sum than that for which the said member's life is
insured . This fund shall likewise be under the super-
vision of the Insurance Commissioner , and at each
examination of the affairsof this society it shall be
his duty to see that the said society has charged,
received and has on hand the sums necessary under
this charter to fulfill its contracts . Should he find
that they have not so charged,received and have on

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 269
hand as above, it shallbe his duty to requirethe said
societywithin one hundred and twenty (120) days to
make good such deficiency, and failingto do so, the
saidsocietyshallhave no furtherpower to make such
endowment contracts: Provided ,That each netendow-
ment premium shall be deposited with some trust
company , or other similarinstitution, to be held in
trustby ituntilinvestedin accordancewith the insur-
ance laws of this Commonwealth as above provided,
and to be applied and used only in carrying out the
contractmade between the societyand any member
holdingan endowment certificate. With the original
certificate, and on every renewal receiptforsame , the
trustcompany aforesaidshallcertifyon said original
certificateor receiptthat they have on deposit,subject
to investment as hereinbefore described , the net pre-
mium called for in such certificateor receipt. Should
any personholding both a lifeand an endowment cer-
tificateforfeithis lifeinsurance in said society, he
shallalsoforfeithis endowment certificate; but in such
a case the said societyshallgive to such member a
paid-up endowment certificate(if he shallhave made
two or more payments ) for as many proportionate
parts of his original certificateas he shall have made
payments, which paid-up certificateshallbe payable
upon same terms and conditionsas the originalcertifi-
cate. The endowment funds and mortuary funds of
said societyshall be entirelyseparateand distinct,
and shall not be liable for the claims of each other .
§2. The board of directorsof saidsocietyhereafter
shallbe divided into two classes, so that at each
biennialmeeting of the societyin which the election
of directorsoccurs,as provided in the originalcharter
of saidsociety, one-half of the saidboard of directors
shallbe elected,who shallhold fora term of four(4)
years, or till their successors are elected; and the
board shallbe limitedin number to ten (10) members ,
and five(5) of them shallconstitutea quorum for the
transaction of business . And the said board shall

270 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
have power to fillany vacancy in theirnumber tillthe
next general election of directors. The present board
shalldesignate by vote or by lot,before the general
electionof January , 1888, the five(5) members who
shallhold over for two years from the date of said
election, and the other five(5) shallbe then electedas
herein above directed .
§ 3. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 28,1886.

CHAPTER 864 .

monwealth of Kentucky :

savings and deposit bank at Owensboro , Kentucky ,

Company , with a capitalstock of not lessthan ten

politicand corporate,under the above name and style,
and as such may sue and be sued,plead, answer and

disposeof realand personalestate;receiveon deposit
gold, silver, money and valuables; loan money , buy
and selland discount promissory notes, billsof ex-
change, stocks, bonds , securitiesand evidences of
debt, and do a generalbanking business; have allthe
rightsof a natural person; use and adopt a common
seal and amend or alter the same .
§ 2. George V. Triplett, W. P. Kent and R. S.Trip-
lett, Jr.,are hereby constituteda board of commis-

1
sionersto open books for subscriptionto stock in said
bank ,and when ten thousand dollars($10,000)of stock
shall have been subscribed and paid in, said bank

controlof five(5) directors,who may adopt by -laws
for the management of said bank . Said directors
shallelecta president, vice-president,and such other

CHAPTER 865 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Fayette county to issuebonds .

under the above recited act, caused one hundred
bonds of said county, of the denomination of one
thousand dollarseach, to be issued,bearing date the

276 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
firstday of October, 1882 ; and whereas, sixty of
said bonds have already been paid or provided for,
leaving stilloutstanding twenty due on the firstday
of April, 1886, and twenty due on the firstday of
April, 1887 ; and whereas, it is deemed oppressive
on said county to pay the whole of said bonds at
the time of theirmaturity; therefore, to extend the
time of a portionof saidbonds ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Fayette county, com-
posed of the county judge and a majority of the
justicesof said county, be, and they are hereby,
authorized and empowered to cause to be issued and
sale made of twenty bonds of like denomination,
with interestcoupons attached,in the same form as
the said outstanding bonds ; and which bonds shall
bear such date and be payable at such time and
place as said court may prescribe; the interestcou-
pons payable semi-annually from the date of said
bonds , and not to exceed the rate of six per cent.
per annum ; and if said bonds shallbe sold, it must
be in the same manner as was prescribed in said
firstrecitedact, and provided for as though they
had been issued under the said act; allthe provis-
ions of which , so far as needed to carry out the
objectsof thisact, shallapply to thisact and the
bonds to be issued thereunder,just as though they
had been issued under said first recited act ; the
only objectof this act being to enable said court to
have longer time for the payment of a portion of
said forty thousand dollars.
§ 2. Be itfurtherenacted, that the proceeds of said
new bonds, which may be issued under thisact,shall
be wholly applied to the payment of such portion of
saidfortybonds as the court may deem advisable.

passage . Approved April 28, 1886 .

CHAPTER 866 .

monwealth of Kentucky :

cession; may make allrules,regulationsand by -laws

CHAPTER 867 .
AN ACT to protect the health of citizenson the lines of railroadsin
Grayson county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful for any railroadcom-
pany in Grayson county to allow any ponds of stag-
nant or foulwater to be formed on theirrightof way
in said county by reason of the constructionor excа-
vationsfor the constructionof any railroad.
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of allcompanies now op-
erating railroadsin said county to drain and keep
drained any such ponds .
§ 3.It shallbe the duty of the justicesof the peace
of Grayson county to see that the provisionsof this
act are complied with ; and any justiceof the peace
may notifyany authorizedagent of said company to
drain such pond within ten days from the serviceof
such notice; and upon the failureof said company to
remove said ponds or to provide fortheirdrainage in
ten days afterthe serviceof thisnoticehereinprovided
for, such company shallbe deemed guiltyof a misde-
meanor,and upon indictmentshallbe finedin a sum
not lessthan fiftynor more than one hundred dollars.
§ 4. Any company or corporationviolatingthe pro-
visionsof thisactin any particularshallbe guiltyof
a misdemeanor ,and upon indictment and conviction
shallbe finednot less than fiftynor more than one
hundred dollars .
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April28,1886.

ern ContractCompany ,and under that name may sue

each ; and allstock subscribedfor shallbe paid in at

named incorporators, or theirsurvivors, or a majority

stock .

passage .

CHAPTER 880 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe Manchester,Crooked Creek and Cove Dale
TurnpikeRoad Company ,of Lewis county.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company is hereby createda body -poli-
ticand corporate, under the name and styleof " The
Manchester , Crooked Creek and Cove Dale Turnpike
Road Company , of Lewis county," forthe purpose of
constructinga macadamized turnpike road from a
point of the Concord and TollesboroTurnpike , near
Cove Dale post-office, thence the most practicable
route down Doyle's Fork and Crooked creek to a
point near the store of James G. Wilson , tapping
the Manchester and Sand HillTurnpike at said point.
§ 2. The capital shall not exceed two hundred
thousand dollars,to be made up by the subscription
stock from Lewis county now authorized by law of
one thousand dollarsper mile, and private stock in
shares of twenty -fivedollars each .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 299
§3 . The following citizensshallconstitutethe in-Incorporators.
corporatorsand commissioners to solicitstock, or so
many of them as will act: A. M. Long , John M.
Myers , Amos Means , George S. Doyle , James H.
Garrett and James G. Wilson .
§4. The commissioners (or any three who will act)
shallprocure suitablebooks in which the stockhold-
ers shall enter into the following obligation, to wit :
" We , whose names are hereunto annexed , promise
to pay to the president and directorsof the Man-
chester,Crooked Creek and Cove Dale Turnpike Road
Company the sum of twenty-fivedollarsforeach and
every share of stock setopposite our names ,in such
proportionsor sums and at such times and places
as the said directorsand presidentmay direct."
§5 . That the stockholders shall be entitled to one
vote for every share of stock paid up held by them ,
and when any stockholder desires, he may vote by
proxy in writingat any and allelectionsfor officers
on said road .
§ 6. That a board of five (5) directorsshall be
elected to conduct the business of said turnpike
company ; the election to be held at some con-
venient point along the lineof said road afterten
days' public notice; the said board of directorsto
immediately organize for business by the electionof
one of their number for presidentand a secretary
and treasurer, the treasurerto give a good and suf-
ficientbond for the faithful performance of his
duties .
§7. The presidentand directorsshallhave power
to fix, grade , locate, build and establish said road
between the points designated, and may acquire by
purchase,gift,relinquishment, writ of ad quod dam-
num , deed or otherwise, the right of way for the
road; the width, metal, etc., to be fixed by said
board of directorsand the aid of a practicalen-
gineer.

300 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§8 . That the county court of Lewis county shall
subscribe stock at the rate of one thousand dollars per
mile,as per provisionsof the law authorizingsaid sub-
scription.

year from the date of their election or until their suc-
cessorsare duly electedand qualified.

cretion,have the authorityto erecta toll-gate or gates
on said road at such placesas they may deem proper ;

sons travelingon said road at the ratesnow fixed by

Kentucky or the United States.

do all other acts and things necessaryto be done to
locateand establishand complete saidturnpike road .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 1, 1886 .

of said city shall be returned to the citycouncil of

CHAPTER 885 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof coloredcommon schoolsof Floyd county ,and
to authorize the superintendent and board of examiners of common
schoolsof saidcounty " to issuespeciallicenseto persons to teach col-
ored common schoolsof said county."
WHEREAS , The colored people of Floyd county
have been unable to obtain teachers to teach their
public schoolsin said county sincethe revised com-
mon schoollaw went intoeffect,caused by therebeing
no coloredteacherswho was livingin said county, or
near thereto, who could obtain certificatesunder the
rigid examination which the present common school
law requires; and whereas, it is right that they
should have common schools, as now provided for
other citizensunder existinglaws,that they may be ,
come cultured and enabled to more fullydischarge
their duties as such citizens; therefore,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 307
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county superintendent of common
schoolsof Floyd county and hisboard of examiners
be, and they are hereby, authorized and empowered
to issuespeciallicenseto a person who cannot have
theexamination required by the common school law,
for the purpose of teachingany coloredcommon
schoolin any common school districtin said county ,
when it shall appear to the satisfactionof said super-
intendent and his said board of examiners that said
person so licensedis qualifiedto teach the colored
pupilsresidingin said common school districts, and
therecannot be a qualifiedteacher had to teach the
same .
§2 . That the provisionsof thisact shallonly apply
and extend to and including the school year ending
June the thirtieth, one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-eight(1888).
§3 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 1, 1886.

vided , shall be elected by the stockholders of the class

dollarsor lessthan twenty-fivecents. Installmentsor
dues may be requireddaily, weekly, semi-monthly
or monthly,as may be fixedby the board of direct-
ors . Shares may be fixedat not lessthan one hun-

§ 4. Provision may be made in the by -laws, if

CHAPTER 888 .

Hickman creek, in Jessamine county .

monwealth of Kentucky :

mit the slopor offalfrom hisdistilleryto flowinto

CHAPTER 890 .
AN ACT to incorporate the LouisvilleDetective Agency .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Gus Rosenberg , Thomas Shepard and
Wm . Coffey be, and they are hereby, incorporated,
under the name of the LouisvilleDetectiveAgency .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 311
§2 . Said corporation shall exist for the period of
twenty -fiveyears, and during that time shallhave
the right to sue and be sued, contractand be con-
tracted with, and have all the powers incidentalto
corporate bodies .

searching for and arrestingcriminals, ferretingout
crimes,and other detectivework of a general nature
and pertainingto the administrationof law in all or
any of its branches .
§ 4. The principalofficeof said corporation shall
be in the cityof Louisville, Kentucky ; but said cor-
porationshallhave power to arrestcriminalsin any

They shallbe governed in all respectsby the laws

vided .

rectors for said corporation for the term of one
year, and thereafter a board of three directors shall

cessors are elected and installed .

ident, secretaryand treasurer; they shallbe elected

passage .
Approved May 1,1886.

CHAPTER 902 .
AN ACT in relationto the moving of tractionand portable engines
over and along the roads and passways of Union county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . Every person using, operating or moving any
tractionengine or any portable engine weighing more
than fivethousand pounds,on or overand alongany
public road in thisState, shallprovide and use at and
on every bridge,over and across which said engine
may pass,timbersthree inches in thicknessby twelve
inches in width , which timbers shall be laid the
entire length of such bridge, and the wheels of said
engine and the wheels of any machine , millor vehicle
connected therewith,shallpass on and along such tim-
bers in crossingsuch bridge; and every person who
shallfailto comply with the provisionsof thissection
shall be fined not less than ten dollars nor more than
fiftydollarsforeach offense.
§2 . Any person using, operating or moving any
traction or portable engine on or along any public
road or private passway or lane in this State, who
shall sufferor permit the whistle of such engine to
blow while on said road, lane or passway , shallbe
fined not lessthan ten nor more than twenty dollars
for each offense .
§3 . Every person using, operating or moving any
tractionor portable engine, moved or hauled in whole
or in part by steam power , on or along any public
road of thisState,shallsend and keep a messenger at
leastone hundred yards in advance of such engine, to
warn allpersons of itsapproach ; and any person fail-
ing to comply with the provisionsof this sectionshall
be fined not lessthan ten nor more than fiftydollars
for each offense .
22- Local Laws , vol.2.

338 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. It shall be the duty of every person using .
operatingor moving any tractionor portableengine,
which is moved or hauled in whole or in part by
steam power , on or along any public road or private
lane or passway in this State, to immediately stop
such engine on the approach of any wagon , carriage,
buggy or othervehicle, and if requestedby the driver
or occupant of such vehicle,to assisthim or them in
passing the engine. And for failingto comply with
the requirementsof thissection, the person so offend-
ing shallbe finednot lessthan ten nor more than fifty
dollars for each offense .
§5 . Any person who ,in violationor disregard of
the firstsectionof this act,shall move any traction
engine or any portable engine weighing over five
thousand pounds over or across any bridge on any
public road in this State, whereby such bridge is
broken or torn up , shall,for each offense, be liable
to county wherein such bridge issituatedfordamage
done to the bridge, to be recovered by action in the
name of and forthe benefitof the county courtin any
courtof competent jurisdiction.
§ 6. All prosecutionsforviolationsof sections1,2,
3 and 4 of thisact shallbe by warrant issued by and
returnablebeforeany justiceof the peace, any police
judge, or the county judge of the county wherein the
offense was committed .
§ 7. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after its passage, and shallapply only to the
county of Union .
Approved May 1, 1886.

CHAPTER 930 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Salt River and Timber Creek Turnpike
Road Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company be,and the same ishereby, in-
corporated, to constructa turnpike road from the ter-
minus of the Camden and Van Buren Turnpike Road ,
inAnderson county, to crossTimber creek, near Mrs.
Ramsey's , and to cross Salt river, near the Croutch
house ; thence to Van Buren , under the name and
style of the Salt River and Timber Creek Turnpike
Road Company ; and by that name and stylemay sue
and be sued, contractand be contractedwith, plead
and be impleaded, and use and have a common seal,
and alterand amend same at pleasure .
§ 2. The capitalstockof saidcompany shallbe fixed
by said company , to be increasedor diminished at the
pleasureof the company ,and to be dividedintoshares
of such an amount as the company may deem best.
§3 . That John Green , John C. Drake , Jacob M.
Snider, Z. G. Mitton and Ben Hardin Thomas are
hereby appointed commissioners, whose duty it shall
be to open books forthe subscriptionof stock at such
places as said commissioners may designate; and as
soon as such an amount as the commissioners may
deem necessaryand sufficientissubscribed, they shall
givetendays'notice,by writtennoticespostedatnot
lessthan twenty of the most public places along the

376 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
proposed routeof saidroad,of a meeting of the stock-
holders, for the purpose of electinga president and
fivedirectorsfor said company , a majority of whom
shallbe competent to do business,and to do allthings
authorizedby thisact to be done by the president and
directorsof saidcompany ; and the management of the
fiscaland prudential affairsof said company shall be
confidedto saidpresidentand directorsand theirsuc-
cessorsin office,to be chosen annually at such times
and places as the said presidentand directorsfrom
time to time may appoint, and who shallcontinue in
officeforone year and untiltheirsuccessorsareelected
and qualified.
§ 4. That no person shall be eligibleas president
or directorof said company who is not the owner
in his own name of one or more shares of stock in
said road .
§ 5. The president and directorsshallhave power
to appoint a treasurer, gate-keepers and all other
officersand agents deemed neccessaryto effect the
purposes of this act, and to remove the same at
pleasure; they shall have power to require of the
treasurer,and allother officersand agents appointed
by them , bond with good security, approved by
them , in such penaltiesas they may require, condi-
tioned for the faithfulperformance of the duties
incumbent on them as such .
§ 6. The commissioners herein appointed shall pro-
cure a book or books , and the subscribersto the stock
of said company shall enter into the following obli-
gation in said book : We , whose names are hereto
subscribed, do promise to pay the president and di-
rectors of the Salt River and Timber Creek Turnpike
Road Company the amounts set opposite our names ,
in such manner and proportion and at such times as
shallbe by them required,under the law incorporat-
ing said company , to be collectedas otherdebts.
Witness our hands this
-
day of - 188- .

§7 . That the width of said road,the width of said
road-bed , the width of the metal on said road , and
thegrade of said road,shallbe regulatedby the pres-
ident and directorsof said company ; and that as
soon as one (1) mile continuous of said road is com-
pleted,the company may erecta toll-gate and charge
tollsthereat at a rate of tollnot exceeding that now
authorizedby law.
§ 8. That said company shall have the right and
power to acquirethe rightof way for said road, and
of earth, stone or gravel or timber for itsconstruc-
tion, by voluntary concession and release, and by
privatecontractwith the land-holders or by writ of
ad quod damnum as prescribedby law.
§ 9. Said company are also empowered to build
and construct a bridge across Salt river, and may
charge tollsfor crossing said bridge at the same
rateas is now allowed by law for travel over five
miles of turnpike road, and have the same rightsto
acquireand condemn property for said bridge pur-
poses as for road purposes .
§ 10. When the stockholders shall have made full
payment of his subscriptionfor stock,itshallbe the
duty of the president, over his signature, to issue
certificatesof stock to allpersons entitledto the same ,
attestedby the secretaryof the company .
§ 11. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 7, 1886.
CHAPTER 931 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe LittleMount Cemetery Company , in

monwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1.That JesseMaratta, Frank Davis, W. S. Allen,
andtwo otherpersonstobe designatedby them,and

theirsuccessors, be, and the same are hereby , incor-
porated under the name of the LittleMount Cemetery
Company , and under that name they may sue and be
sued, contractand be contracted with, and perform
such other duties and exercise such other privileges
as are usuallyconferredon similarcorporations.

transactbusiness when and so soon as said corpora-
tors shall meet and elect out of their number three
directors, who shall have the full management and
controlof the affairsof the company , subject to the
control of the stockholders as hereinafter stated . The
firstboard of directors shall hold their offices for one
year, and untiltheirsuccessorsare electedand quali-
fied,and annually afterthe date of the firstelection
three directorsshall be elected by the stockholders .
§ 3. The board of directors shall, out of their num-
ber, annually electa president, secretaryand treas-
urer, who shallhold their officesfor one year, and
untiltheirsuccessorsare electedand qualified. The
treasurershallexecute a bond to the company ,with
approved security,forthe faithfulperformance of his
duties, and that he will account for and pay over to
