quired with which he has been intrusted, and said
bond shallbe deliveredto the Spencer county clerk
forsafe-keeping ; and any person injured may sue on
saidbond , and recoversuch damages as he may have
sustainedby any failureof the treasurerto perform
his duties . The treasurer shall collect and distribute
the funds of the company ,and keep a faithfulaccount
of same .
§4. The purpose of thisincorporationisto establish,
preserve and beautifya cemetery near LittleMount,
Spencer county, Kentucky ; and this company shall
have power to apply to the courtsof Spencer county
and have such lands condemned for the use of the com-
pany as itmay deem proper, not exceeding ten acres.
The landshall,ifitcannotbe acquiredby contract,be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 379
condemned in the same manner and before the same
courts and with same proceedingsasisnow provided by
law for the condemnation of lands for the use of rail-
road and turnpike companies . When the company
shallhave selectedthe locationof the cemetery, and
so soon as the price to be paid the owner or owners
has been fixed by agreement between the partiesor
by the judgment of the court,as providedhereinwhen
no agreement can be made ,the company may proceed,
before paying forthe lands,to have the land surveyed
and laidoffinto lotsand roads and drives, and may
proceed to advertisesaid lots for sale, in such por-
tions thereofas they may deem proper, and on such
terms as may be fixed by the directors; and may sell
the same on condition that enough such sales are
made to purchase the land and establishthe cemetery ;
and when a sufficientnumber of salesare so made ,
they may be made absolute, and the proceeds col-
lected and applied to the payment of the lands and
ornamenting and beautifying the grounds, and for
such other purposes as the directorsmay prescribe
incident to the organization. But no soil shall be
broken or any possession of any owner interfered
with until a sufficient number of sales shall have been
made to securethe purchase of the lands designated,
and provided the incorporatorsnamed herein shallbe
personallybound forsuch damages as any owner may
sustainby any necessaryintrusionupon hispremises,
in the event the cemetery isnot locatedthereon.
§ 5. The purchasers of the lots, and those who in-
vest theirfunds in the enterpriseby giftor donation,
and theirpriviesin estateor otherwise, shallbe the
stockholders , and shall be entitled to one vote for
every fivedollarsthus invested; and certificatesof
stock may be issuedby the directorsto any such per-
son, and the company is authorizedto receivedona-
tionsand giftsforthe purposes of thisact.
§ 6. Obligationsof the company must be, if in
writing, signedby the presidentand secretary; and

380 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
certificatesof purchase so signed may be given to
purchasers of lots, designating number and other
description, which may be recorded in the county
clerk'sofficeas evidenceof title,and preservedby the
secretary. The board of directors shall have full
power of regulatingthe placesand manner of inter-
ment ,in order to preserveharmony and uniformity.
and have fullpower of rejectingany bidder for lots,
so as to confinecolored people to one portion and
white people to another, and may vitiateand annul
any saleor transferof lotsin violationof such regu-
lations, and may exercise a general control of the en-
tire inclosure .
§ 7. This act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage .
Approved May 7, 1886.

§ 9. It shall be the duty of the county judge of
Kenton county, upon a petitionsigned by ten free-
holders of either of said districts, asking that this
act shallbe submitted to the legal qualifiedvoters
of said districtfor adoption or rejection, at least
thirtydays before said election, to directthe sheriff
of Kenton county, by an order duly entered on the
order book of the county court, to open a poll in
said district or districts, at the election mentioned
in the petition, which electionmust be some regular
August election, to take the sense of the voters in
said districtor districtsmentioned , whether they are
in favor of the adoption or the repeal of this act .
If the object of the vote is to adopt this act, the
clerk of said electionshallpropound to the voter,
"Are you in favor of the stock law ?" and his vote
shallbe recordedyes or no as directedby saidvoter ;
and if itappear that the greater number have voted
" yes," this act shall take effectin five days after
said election. If at any electionthis act is adopted
and becomes a law , and there is an order made for
an electionto take the vote of the people in said dis-
trictor districts,upon the repeal of said act, then it
shallbe the duty of the clerkof said electionto pro-
pose to each voterthe followingquestion,"Are you in
favorof repealingthe stock law ?" and he shallrecord
yes or no as directedby said voter; and if it appear
that the greaternumber have voted " yes," then this
act shallbe repealed and take effectfivedays after
said election, and the vote shall be counted, can-
vassed and certifiedby the county board of canvass-
ers .

CHAPTER 970 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Victor Emmanuel Club of Louisville.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Lawrence Gatto, W. R. Campbell and
J. S. Miller, theirassociates, successorsand assigns,
be, and they are hereby, created a body -politicand
corporate, under the name and style of the Victor
Emmanuel Club of Louisville, and by that name
shallhave perpetualsuccession,and power to contract
and be contracted with , sue and be sued , and do busi-
ness generally.
§2 . The capital stock of said club shall be one
thousand dollars, divided into shares of ten dollars
each . Each owner of a share of said stock shall
thereby become a member of said club, and in the
event of a resignationor expulsion of any member
of said club , his stock shall revert to the club .
§3 . The said club shallbe located in the city of
Louisville,Kentucky , and shallhave power and it is

456 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
hereby authorizedto establish, furnish and maintain
a suitableclub-house for the use, convenience and
benefitof itsmembers ,and for the purpose of social
enjoyment,improvement and hospitalityto members
and strangers, and for that purpose may purchase,
build or rent suitableland and buildingsin said city.
The said club may take and hold by gift, purchase
or devise personal or real estate to the value of ten
thousanddollars($10,000),but saidpropertyshallbe
used for the purposes of said corporation alone.
Said club may adopt such constitutionand by -laws
as they may deem necessaryand proper.
§4.The affairsof saidclubshallbe managed and
controlledby three directors, one of whom shall be
presidentof said club, to be electedat such time and
forsuch term as may be prescribedby the by -laws of

scribedby the by -laws thereof.
§ 5. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 7, 1886.

directors .

until the firstTuesday in May ,1887, when the first
electionfordirectorsshalltake place; and as soon as
ten thousand ($10,000) dollarsof itscapitalstock has

allpowers and transactallbusinesshereinauthorized.

for and to build jails,bridges,or other structures,or

within the Stateof Kentucky or elsewhere, and shall

CHAPTER 981 .
AN ACT to empower the county courtsof Ballard and Carlislecounties
tolevy an ad valoremtax torepairand buildbridgesinsaidcounties,
and to elector appoint bridge commissioners.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county courtsof Ballard and Carlisle
counties, a majority of the justicesof the peace of
said countiesbeing presentand concurringtherein,be,
and is hereby , authorized and empowered to levy at
each annual session an ad valorem tax of not exceed-
ing ten cents on each one hundred dollars' worth of
taxableproperty insaidcounties;and the money ,when
so raised, to be used in buildingand repairingbridges
in said counties .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of the sheriffsof said
countiesto collectsaid taxes, and for collectingsame
they shall be allowed the same commision now allowed
them by law for collectingthe revenue tax ; but be-
fore they shallproceed to collectthe tax provided for
in the firstsectionof thisact they shallexecute bond ,
with good and approved surety or sureties,in the said
county courts, for the faithfulperformance of their
duties in collecting,accounting for and paying over
the said taxes in due time, as the said county courts
may direct; and for any violationof said bonds suit
may be institutedin any courtin saidcountieshaving
jurisdictionin said cases.
§ 3. The county courtsof the saidcountiesmay , at
any regularterm of saidcourt, a majority of the jus-
tices concurring therein, elect or appoint a bridge
commissioner for theirrespectivecounties,who shall
hold his officefor the term of one year, and whose
duty shallbe to attend to the lettingout of the build-
ing or repairingof any and allbridgesin theirrespect-
ive countiesordered builtor repaired by said courts;
30- Local Laws , vol. 2.

466 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and before said commissioner shall enter upon the
duties of his officehe shallbe duly sworn and give
bond for the faithfuldischarge of his duties .
§ 4. The county courtsshallallowthe commissioners
electedor appointed in this act a reasonablefee for
their services,to be paid out of the county levy of
their respective counties.
§ 5. This act shallbe in forcefrom the thirdday of
May , 1886 .
Approved May 7, 1886.
CHAPTER 982 .
AN ACT to prevent stock from running at large on the turnpike
roads in Nicholas county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shallbe unlawful for any stock to run
at largeon any turnpike road in Nicholas county.
§2 . Any person or persons,being the owners of or
having the custody of stock of any kind, who shall
suffer or permit any of them to run at large on any
portionof the linesof the turnpike roads that liein
Nicholas county, shallbe fined,for each and every
offense, not less than two dollars nor more than
twenty dollars for each and every offense, to be
recovered by warrant in the name of the Common-
wealth beforeany judge or justiceof the peace which
the turnpike road runs,or the nearestjudge or any
justiceof said turnpike road.
§3 . Unless said fineispaid or replevied, the stock
so running at large shall be subjectto the fineand
costs,and an execution shall be levied upon it, and
the officershall proceed to collect same as in case of
other executions. The fineso paid shall go to the
trusteeof the jury fund of Nicholas county .
§ 4. This act shall take effectand be in force from
the firstday of June , 1886, and noticeshallbe posted

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 467
at two of the most public places on the lines of said
turnpike roads for two weeks beforethe day this law
takes effect. Any road failingto post notices, this
law shall not take effect until said notices have been
posted for twenty days .
Approved May 7,1886.
CHAPTER 983 .

ituous,vinousor malt liquors,orany mixturethereof,upon the day of
any electionor politicalconvention,inthecountiesofWayne and Rus-
sell .

vinous or malt liquors, or any mixture thereof,either
directly or indirectly, on the day of any primary ,
school,county, Stateor nationalelection, or upon the
day of any politicalconvention, in the countiesof
Wayne and Russell .
§2 . Any person or persons who shall violatethe
provisionsof section1 of thisact shallbe guiltyof a
misdemeanor , and ,upon convictionthereof, shall be
finednot lessthan fivedollarsnor more than twenty-
fivedollarsfor each offense, to be recovered by pro-
ceedings in any court of competent jurisdiction.
§ 3. The provisionsof this act shall not apply to
salesmade by wholesaledealersin quantitiesnot less
than thirtygallons.
§ 4. This act shalltake effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage.
Approved May 7,1886 .

CHAPTER 987 .
AN ACT forthebenefitofthe owner orlesseeof the ferryat Ashland,
Kentucky , and providingpenaltiesfora violationof the provisionsof
same .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person or cor-
poration,within one and a half miles each way from
the establishedferryat Ashland ,over the Ohio river,
to keep or maintain a ferryover or across said river,
or keep a boat, skiffor other water craft, with a hand
or hands , tobe used forthe purpose of ferrying, or in
which they may for compensation or otherwise, trans-
port or carry any horse,wagon ,cart, carriage, person
or other thing whatever , over or across said river
within the limitsaforesaid, nor shall any person or
corporationfurnish,hireor loan to another any boat,
skiffor otherwater craftto be used for ferryingany
person or other thing over said river within the limits
aforesaid, with or without compensation: Provided,
That the provisionsof thisact shall not apply to the
Ciota Valley Railroad, or to anything by them trans-
ferredover saidriver, in the regular exerciseof their
franchiseat saidpoint;nor shallthe provisionsof this
act apply to any residentowner of a skiffor other
water craft, when used by him or them in ferrying
any member of his family or employe over said river,
withinthe limitsaforesaid, or forferryingsuppliesto
them,or eitherof them ,or for using the same in any
way for the use of his own family.
§2. Any person or persons violatingthe provisions
ofthisact shall, foreach offense,be finedany sum not
lessthan ten or over twenty dollars, recoverablebe-
forethemayor ofthe cityof Ashland,or any justice
ofthepeace therein, upon a warrant in the name of
the owner or lesseeof said establishedferry, which
may be triedat any time.
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage . Approved May 7, 1886.

passage .

CHAPTER 992 .
AN ACT toprovidefora registrationof votersin the cityof Paducah.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Officers.
§ 1. That the councilof the cityof Paducah shall,
at theirfirstregularmeeting in January of each year,
elect four sober and discreet citizens in each ward of
the cityof Paducah , two to act as judges of registra-
tion, one as clerkof registration,and one as marshal
of registrationin the ward for which they are elected.
No person shallbe eligibleas judge,clerk or marshal
who is not a voter of the ward in which he is elected,
and a housekeeper in the ward forwhich he iselected.
When thereare two distinctpoliticalpartiessaid offi-
cersof registrationshallbe so selectedas that one of
the judges in each ward shallbe of one politicalparty
and the other of the opposite politicalparty. They
shall serve for one year from the time of theirelection;
and each officerof registration,before entering upon
the discharge of his duties, shalltake the oath pre-
scribedby section1,article8,of the StateConstitution,
and during the period of his term of serviceshallbe
excused from jury duty .
§ 2. The councilclerkshall, at leastfivedays before
the registrationin each year, give each registration
officerwritten notice of his election. Should the coun-
cilfailto elect, or should all or any of the officers
electedfailto attendat the placeof registrationfor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 483
one hour afterthe time for commencing the registra-
tion,or should allor any of said officersrefuseor de-
clineto act, then the mayor of the city of Paducah
shallappointofficersto actin theirstead for that reg-
istration, and may administer to them the necessary
oath of office.
§3. Officersof registrationshallhave the same power
to preserve order at the places of registration as is
exercisedby officersof electionsat the polls in State
elections. If the officersof registrationentertainany
doubt as to whether or not any person offeringfor
registrationis entitledto such registration, or if any
one'sright to registeris challenged, citizensmay be
calledin,not exceeding threein number , who shallbe
examined under oath touching the qualificationsof
suchperson who offersto register,which oath may be
administeredto any the persons so examined by any
oneofthejudges of registration.
Forms .
§4. It shallbe the duty of the clerkof the council
toprepare the proper forms and cause to be printed
two registrationbooks for each ward in the city of
Paducah, one to be kept in his officeto be used as
provided in section 9 of this act, and the other
furnishedto the clerks of registrationand election
beforeeach registrationor electionday , as herein-
afterprovided. Said books shallbe so arranged as
thatthe names of votersmay be registeredalphabet-
ically,and shallbe ruled and headed as follows:
AugustElection
MarchElection
Color
Residence
Name
REMARKS .

484 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Registration .
§5 . The officersof registrationshall attend at the
voting places of their respectivewards on the first
Monday in January of each year,except in 1887 ,when
they shallattend on the firstMonday after their elec-
tion and on the next succeedingday from the hours
of six in the morning untileightin the evening ,and
shall record in the registrationbook , which shallbe
furnishedby the clerkof the councilto each registra-
tion clerk, a listof such qualifiedvotersof the ward
as may apply for registration. Said list of voters
shall be in alphabeticalorder, and shall show the
name of the person registered,the name of the street
or alley, describing brieflythe house in which he
lives,givingthe streetand between what two streets
itislocated. No person shallbe registeredwho does
not personallyappear before the registrationofficers;
and ifhe be not personallyknown to one of the offi-
cers, or if any bystander shalldemand it, he shallbe
sworn by one of the officersand interrogatedby him,
or by such bystander, touching his qualificationsas
provided in this act. Opposite the name of each per-
son so sworn the clerk shall write the word " sworn ,"
which entry shall be prima facie evidence of such
swearing in any prosecutionunder thisact. Any per-
son registeredmay vote at allelectionsuntilthe next
registration,unlesshe becomes disqualifiedafterregis-
tering.
Qualifications.
§6. Every personshallbe entitledto be registered
who would be entitled to vote at the next succeeding
March election; that is to say, every male citizen
who , on that day, shallhave attainedthe age of
twenty-one years,and shallhave residedin theState
two years, or in the county one year and sixty days
in the ward in which he offersto register.
Aliens .
§ 7. Any alienpossessingallthe qualificationsofa

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 485
voter, named in section6 of thisact,except citizen-
ship, shallbe entitledto be registered, but the clerk
shallwrite opositehis name , in the column headed
" Remarks ," the words " not naturalized; " and such
alienshallnot be entitledto vote at any electionheld
under that registration, unlesshe shallproduce to
theofficersof electionhis naturalizationpapers in due
form of law .
Mode of Registering.
§8 . In making the registration, the clerk shallact
as the recording officer, and the judges shalldecide
allquestionsrelatingto the qualificationsof persons
offeringto be registered,exceptthat,incaseof a dif-
ferenceof opinion between the judges,the clerk shall
have the casting vote. It shall be the duty of the
clerkto number , consecutively, the names recorded
under each letterof the alphabet as they are taken
down , and at the close of the registrationhe shall
sign his name as clerk after the last name recorded
under each letteras aforesaid, in such manner as that
no more names can be recorded above his, and shall
footup and certifyin the back of the registration
book the whole number of names recorded at that
registration; and this certificateshall be signed by
allthe officersof registrationbeforeleavingthe place
of registration, and in the presenceof bystanders
any two of whom may ,ifthey desire,sign theirnames
aswitnessesthereto. In additionto the requirements
above, the clerk shallsign his name as such clerk at
thefootof each page .
Return and Custody of the Books .
§9 . On the day followingthe close of the registra-
tion,theregistrationclerksshalldeliverthe registra-
tionbooks into the hands of the clerk of the council,
and take his receipttherefor. It shallbe the duty of
theclerkof the councilto keep said books safelyin
hisoffice, and not suffer the same to be taken there-
from, except as provided in this act. He shallalso

486 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
cause to be made one copy of each registrationbook in
the blank book retainedby him as provided in section
4 of thisact, which shallbe kept in his office, and not
be taken therefrom for any purpose . In case of loss
of any registrationbook , a copy shallbe made by the
clerk of the council from the copy retained in his
office,which copy shallbe used in registrationsand
electionswith the same effectas the original. The
clerk of the council shallpermit any citizenat any
time to copy any registrationbook without fee or
charge,and he shall also furnishcopies at the same
rateas provided in section17 of this act for copying
the book kept in his office. Copies furnished to citi-
zens under thissectionshallbe paid for in cash by the
person orderingthem .
Removals .
§ 10. Any person removing from one ward toanother
afterhaving been registeredfor that year,may apply
to the clerk of registration of the ward from which
he removes, on a registrationday ,and have his name
canceledoffby writingoppositeto itthe word " re-
moved ," and thereupon said clerk shall give him a
certificatein the followingform : " This is to certify
that ward to thehas removed from the
ward ,and that hisregistrationhas been canceled.
(Signed) — , clerk." Upon presentingsaidcer-
tificateto the officersof registrationof the ward to
which he has removed , he shall be entitled to be
thereregistered, if he possessthe other qualifications
named in section 6 of this act ; and no person remov-
ing, after being registered, shall be registeredin
another ward , without the production of such a cer-
tificate .
Striking Off Names .
§ 11. Any votermay ,by giving fivedays' notice, in
writing, addressed to, and served on, any person
whose name has been registered, move the councilof
the cityof Paducah , at itsnext regular meeting, not

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 487
!
less than five days after service of said notice, to
strike the name of such person from the register.
The council shallthereupon,at once, appoint a com-
mittee, consistingof three members of the council,
who shallhear proof on the question of strikingthe
name of such person from the register; and if the
committee shallbe of opinion that the person regis-
tered is not lawfully entitledto be registered, after
hearing the proof, the committee shall direct the
clerk of the council to strike his name from the
registerby insertingopposite his name the words ,
" Stricken by order of the council committee, — ,
clerk of the council." The person having the notice
served and the person upon whom it is served,may
each introduce not exceeding two witnesses on a side.
Within two days afteritsappointment by the council,
the committee shall hear proof of the parties on the
question of registration, and shall make report in
writing of the resultof itsaction,which reportshall
be filed with the clerk of the council within one week
after the appointment of the committee. If the per-
son upon whom noticeis attempted to be served can
not be found , a return of that fact, sworn to, and
signed by the person who endeavored to serveit,shall
be presentedto the councilat itsfirstregularmeeting,
not lessthan fivedays afterthe date of the notice,
accompanied by motion in writingto strikethe name
of the person in question from the register, and the
council,at itsnext regular meeting afterthe filingof
the motion , shall order the council clerk to insert on
the register, opposite the name of the person whose
qualificationto registeris questioned,the following:
" doubtful, by order of the council. -- , clerk of
the council;" and when ,at an election,such person
whose name is marked doubtful shall offerto vote,
he shallbe sworn , and his right to vote investigated
fully.
§ 12. On Saturday previous to the firstMonday in
March , 1887, and on the juridicalday previous to

488 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
every electionto be held,or vote of the people to be
taken thereafterin the cityof Paducah , for the elec-
tion of city officers, the clerk of the council shall
deliverto the clerks of the electionthe registration
books for theirseveralwards, togetherwith the poll-
books, which said registrationand poll-books shallbe
produced by said clerks at their severalwards when
the pollsare opened on the day of election. At said
electionthe vote of no person shall be received unless
the name of the person offeringtovote ison the regis-
try, or unlesshe produce beforethe officersof election
his writtenaffidavitsignedby him, statingthathe
was necessarilyabsent from the city at the time the
registrationof votersin the citywas taken, or thathe
was illand unable to attend, or that some named
member of his family was illduring such absence or
illnesscovering the period allowed for registration,
and statingfactsshowing him to be a qualifiedvoter
of theward inwhich he claimstherighttovote,and
also the facts in reference to his residence required to
be entered on the register, and shallalso prove, by
the writtenaffidavitof a registeredvoterof the ward ,
who is a householder, that he knows such person
to be a qualified voter, and stating the facts in
referenceto such person's place of residence re-
quired to be entered on the register. Said affi-
davit shall be returned by the officersof elec-
tion to the clerk of the council, indorsed as
follows : " Filed before us this
18- .
day of ,
-,judges;" and shallbe kept on file
in his officeby the clerkof the council. Any vote
received by the officersof election in contravention of
thisact shallbe void,and shallbe rejectedfrom the
count. The officersof electionshall, when a vote is
cast,mark oppositethe name of the person voting, in
the column of the registrationbook provided for that

False Registration .
§13. Any person who shall cause himself to be
registeredin more than one ward , otherwise than is
providedin section10 of this act,or more than once
inthe same ward ,or who shall cause himself to be
registered, knowing thathe is not 21 years of age, or
knowing that he is not lawfully entitledto registra-
tion; and any person who shallaidor abetin the com-
missionof any of saidacts,shallbe deemed guiltyof
amisdemeanor, and shallbe imprisoned in the county
jailnot less than one nor more than twelve months ,
orconfinedin the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor
more than two years,in the discretionof the jury.
False Registration Books .
§14. Any officerof registrationor otherperson who
shallunlawfully alterany registrationbook , or add
any name thereto,or who shallwillfullysecrete, sup-
press, or destroyany such book , or who shallmake or
aid in making any false or fraudulent registration
book,shallbe deemed guilty of felony, and shallbe
confinedin the penitentiarynotlessthan one nor more
than fiveyears, and shallforfeitany officehe then
holds, and shall forever be disqualifiedfrom holding
office.
Perjury .
§ 15. Any person who shall willfullymake any
falsestatement, under oath, lawfully administered
under thisact,shallbe deemed guiltyof perjury,and
confinedin the penitentiarynot less than one nor
more than fiveyears .
Obstructing Registration .
§ 16. Any person who ,by himselfor in aidof others,
shallforciblybreak up , or attempt to break up , a

490 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
registrationheld under thisact, or shall forciblypre-
vent,or attempt toprevent,any person from approach-
ing or enteringa place of registrationfor the purpose
of registering, shallbe finednot lessthan one hundred
nor more than fivehundred dollars, or imprisoned
not less than six nor more than twelve months in the
county jail, or both.
Fees .
§ 17. For his services under this act the clerk of the
councilshallreceivethe followingfees, and no other:
For copying the registrylistsrequired to be kept in
his office, or to be used in supplying lostbooks ,the
sum of one cent for each voter whose name iscopied;
for his services under section 11 of this act, the sum
of ten cents for the name of each voter stricken from
the lists; for receiptingto clerks of registrationand
electionfor registrationbooks,the sum of fivecents
foreach book receiptedfor; he shallreceivefor serv-
ing the noticesprovided for in section2 the sum of
twenty-fivecents for each actual service. Officersof
registrationshallreceivetwo dollarsper day for each
day employed in attending at the place of registra-
tion,but clerksof registrationand electionshallnot
be entitledto any pay ,except upon the producing the
receiptof the clerkof the council, which receiptshall
be the voucher of the officerpaying them . For his
servicesin cases arisingunder section11 of this act
each member of the committee shall receive two dol-
larsper day foreach day he actuallyserves. The city
of Paducah shallpay allfeesand expenses incurred
under thisactfor registrations,except in contestsaris-
ing under section11 of thisact; the person by whom
or at whose instancethe noticewas served shall pay
allcostsof the proceeding in such contest, should the
committee declineto strikefrom the registerthe name
of the person upon whom the noticewas served.
§ 18. At any time afterthe second registrationday
and before the next ensuing August or March election

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 491
thereafter,forthe electionof cityofficers,thecouncil
may , in itsdiscretion, have the registeredlistof vot-
ers,includingthe name ,colorand localresidenceof
each voter,published in one of the daily papers of the
city of Paducah , or the councilmay , in itsdiscretion,
have said listpublished in any otherway the council
deem proper .
§ 19. The Paducah citycourt shallhave jurisdiction
by warrant of the trialof any offensemade punish-
able by this act.
§ 20. A copy of this act shall be printed in each
registrationbook made under itsprovisions.
§ 21. This act shalltake effectand be in forcefrom
itspassage .
Approved May 10, 1886.

CHAPTER 995 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous,vinousor malt liquors,or
intoxicatingbeverage,in quantitieslessthan ten gallons,within five
milesof Union College,inKnox county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That from and afterthe passage of this act it
shallbe unlawful for any person tosell,eitherdirectly
or indirectly,in quantitieslessthan tengallons,spirit-
uous, vinous or malt liquors, or intoxicatingbever-
age,within fivemiles of Union College,locatedin the
town of Barbourville,in Knox couuty .
§ 2. Any person violatingthe provisionsof thisact
shall be fined one hundred dollars for each offense .
§ 3. The police judge of Barbourville, the county
judge of Knox county, and any magistrate living
within fivemiles of Union College, shallhave con-
current jurisdiction with the circuit court in the en-
forcement of this act .
§ 4. All laws in conflictwith this act are hereby
repealed ; and this act shall take effectfrom itspas-
sage .
Approved May 7,1886.

CHAPTER 1001 .
AN ACT fixingthe boundary and for the benefitof the school district
No. 67,in Fleming county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the boundary of common school district
No. 67, of Fleming county, shall be as follows, to
wit : Beginning with the farm of James Biddell;
thence down Buchanan creek, including James Rodg-
ers' farm ; thence down said creek to the lower corner
of C. W. Hammond's farm , including said farm ;
thence south,includingJ. R. Crawford's,John Boon's
and A. E. Collins' farms ; thence down Hoover run,
excluding W. S. Price'sfarm, to J. C. Bishop's farm,
includingsame ; thence east, including Mrs. Lavina
Alexandria's, Sanford Gray's,Robert Harper's, Wm .
H. Price's, J. N. Price'sand W. S. Williams' farms,
to the James Biddell farm, including same , or the
place of beginning,includingallthe lands and premi-
ses within said boundary .
§ 2. The superintendent of common schools for
Fleming county shall not change the boundary of
said districtwithin a period of five years after the
passage of this act, unless by the unanimous consent
of the board of trustees for said district.
§3 . A tax not exceeding twenty -five cents on the
one hundred dollars'worth of taxableproperty in said
districtmay be levied in any one year, and for a
period not exceeding three (3) successiveyears, at
any one electionby a majorityof thequalifiedvoters
presentand voting therefor; and those only shall be
deemed qualified to vote who reside in said district,
and are entitled to vote for common school trustees .
§ 4. The following citizens, resident within said dis-
trict, are hereby created a board of trustees : J. R.
Crawford , Wm . S. Williams, and C. W. Hammond ,
who shall hold their officeuntil the firstSaturday in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 507
June ,when the qualifiedvotersof saiddistrictshall
proceed toelectthree(3)trustees,one fora periodof
one year,one fortwo years,and one forthreeyears,or
untiltheirsuccessorsare duly electedand qualified.
§5 . Said trusteesappointed in this act shall have
untilthe fifteenthday of May ,1886,to listallchildren
within school age resident within said district, and to
report to the county superintendent; and all listsof
school children, and assessmentsnow made by other
districtsof said children, is hereby declarednull and
void ; and said county superintendentshallcorrectall
said lists to conform with the new lists and assess-
ments made under this act .
§ 6. It shallbe the duty of the trusteescreatedun .
der thisact to give ten (10) days' noticebefore the
electionin June , 1886,to the voters of said district,
that an electionwillbe held for the purpose of voting
a tax for erecting a new school-house, and for the
election of trustees ; said notice shall be posted in no
less than three (3) prominent places within saiddis-
trict. The board of trusteesfor said districtshall,
within ten days afteran electionvoting a tax,make
out a true and correct list of all the white owners of
propertyinsaiddistrict,and oftheamount and value
of property owned by each subject to taxation for
revenue purposes, and of allthe white tithesresiding
in said district. Said board of trustees shall have full
power to choose the locationof saidschool-house .
§ 7. In addition to the foregoingprovisionforlevy-
ing a tax, the board of trusteesfor saiddistrictmay ,
at itsdiscretion,levya tax each year of two (2) dollars
per head on each white male person of twenty -one
years of age residingin saiddistrict.
§ 8. All the money raisedby taxes voted, levied, as-
sessed and collectedaccordingto the provisionsof this
act shall be expended for nothing else than for the
purchasing of a sitefor a districtschool-house, the
building , repairingor furnishingthe same , and for
fuel and other necessary articlesfor the proper con-

508 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ducting of saidschool; and forfencingand necessary
out-buildings,and forthe betterpayment of the teach-
er or teachersof said school, or for the extension of
the term of the common school in said district for a
period longerthan fivemonths ; or forthe payment of
debts contractedby the trusteesin the interestof the
common school in said district.
§ 9. Said board of trustees shall be controlled and
governed by the common school law , when the same
is not inconsistent with this act .
§ 10. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 7, 1886 .
CHAPTER 1002 .
AN ACT to authorize D. R. Carr, specialjudge of the Barren circuit
court,atitsMarch term,1886,tosign ordersand judgments made by
him at said term of said court .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That whereas, at the March term, 1886, of the
Barren circuitcourt, D. R. Carr was elected and
served as specialjudge thereof, and failedto sign,
during said term of saidcourt,the record of the orders
and judgments made and rendered while he presided
as such specialjudge, he is now empowered and di-
rected to sign said record so made under his direction
while presidingas such specialjudge at said term ,to
have the same forceand effectas ifhe had have signed
the same during the term of said court at which he
presided as special judge thereof, as hereinbefore
stated .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 7 , 1886 .

CHAPTER 1006 .
AN ACT for the benefitof R. J. Thomason , of Franklin county .
WHEREAS , R. J. Thomason , of Franklin county,
was employed by W. T. B. South,warden of thepen-
itentiary, from ninth February untiltwenty-firstof
March ,1886,as night guard in cell-house,at the rate
of fortydollarsper month ; and whereas,when South
vacated the positionas warden , the said Thomason
was not retainedby the present warden , and on his
applyingto the Auditor and secretaryof the Board of
Commissionersforhis pay ,was refusedpayment ; the
reasonassigned was that his appointment as guard
had never been confirmed ; and whereas , the said
Thomason rendered the services, and , according to
the certificateof the warden ,was a good and efficient
officer, and isa poor man unable to render thisservice
to the State without compensation ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor be, and he ishereby, directed
todraw his warrant upon the Treasurer in favor of
the said R. J. Thomason for the sum of fifty-three
dollarsand thirty-threeand one-thirdcents,tobe paid

510 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
out of any money in the treasury not otherwiseap-
propriated .
§ 2. That this act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 7,1886.

ciationas amended is hereby repealed. Sections3,4,

hundred dollars. In grading the license,the general

on every thousand dollarsof yearly salesat whole-
sale; of all other commodities,except grain, cotton,
and provisions, not less than fiftycents on every
thousand dollarsof yearly salesat wholesale of these
articles, and of not lessthan two dollars on every
thousand dollarsof yearly salesat retail; and shall
collect the license tax within the above limits at the
end of the year for the excess of sales over the esti-
mate made at the time of issuingthe license. The sum
of twenty-one hundred dollarsshall, on the firstof
everymonth ,beginning on the firstof June , 1884,be
paid by the sinking fund commissioners into the

lieu of the former school tax on merchandise . For
any theatricalexhibition,lecture,concert, show , cir-
cus,flyingdutchman , menagerie, ball, entertainment
orperformance in the city for pay , such sum , of not
lessthan ten dollars nor more than one hundred dol-
larsa day, as the generalcouncilmay ,by ordinance,
prescribe; but a yearly licensemay be granted to a
theater of not less than two hundred dollars nor more
thanthreehundred dollars. For each vehiclerunning

in said city, not lessthan two nor more than thirty
dollars; and vehicles thus licensed shall not be sub-
jectto an ad valorem tax ; forevery lotteryoffice, two
hundred dollarsper annum ; foreach billiardor pool

twenty nor more than one hundred dollars; for each
tavern,hotel,publicboarding house,or place of public

nor more than seventy-fivedollars; foreach tavern or

CHAPTER 1011 .
AN ACT to authorize the county court of Warren county to subscribe
stockinturnpikeroadsinsaidcounty.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county courtof Warren county be ,and
it is hereby,empowered to pay to any turnpike com-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 517
pany which has been, or may hereafterbe, incorpo-
rated by the county court of said county, or by the
Legislatureof the Stateof Kentucky ,the sum of one
thousand dollars, towards the erectionand completion
of every mile of turnpike which may be made in said
county, inmanner and form, and upon the condition,
and subjectto the limitationsas hereinafterprovided.
§2 . Said county court shallnot pay or advance any
money untilthe company making applicationtherefor
shall have completed one mile of well-constructed and
durable turnpike road, of gravelor stone, as required
by theirrespectivecharters, nor untilsaid company
shall have given in writing a statement showing that
saidcompany has ample means or privatesubscriptions
on hand with which ,and the one thousand dollarsper
mile to be drawn from the county , to construct and
put in operationat leastthreemilesof turnpike road.
Before paying said subscriptionthe said court shall
causesaid road to be carefullyexamined .
§3 . Upon the payment of each one thousand dollars
by the county court, the company to which it is paid
shallissuea certificateof stockto the county of War-
ren for the amount so paid by her ; and said county,
actingthrough the county court, shallparticipatein
the control and management , and share in the divi-
dends of any such road as any otherstockholder.
§ 4. Said county court, in making payments and
taking stock as hereinbeforeprovided,shallnot give
preferenceto any road over any otherroad which isin
processof construction, or isin good faithproposed to
be built; but in taking such stock shallbe as nearly
justto every portionof the county as possible; but in
no event shallsaid county court pay towards, or said
county be a stockholder in, the roads of said county
under the provisionsof this act beyond the sum of
thirtythousand dollars; thissum being in additionto
the amount alreadyvoted by saidcounty.
§ 5. All proceedingstouching subscriptionof stock
by said county shallbe kept of record in the county

518 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
court clerk'soffice, and the county court clerk shall
keep a book in which shall be recorded the date and
amount of each subscriptionof stock by the county of
Warren , in what company , and when said subscrip-
tion was made .
§ 6. All dividends receivedby the county of War-
ren on the stock held by her in any turnpike com-
pany , shallbe paid intothe county treasuryas of the
county named .
§ 7. Before said county court shallmake any sub-
scription of stock to any turnpike road company , as
authorizedby thisact, said court shallsubmit to the
qualified voters of Warren county the question,
whether or not the county of Warren shall subscribe
stock in the turnpike companies in saidcounty. Said
question may be submitted as aforesaidat any time,
afterat leasttwenty days' noticeshall be given of
said election; said noticeto be given by publication
for at leasttwenty days in some weekly newspaper
published in Bowling Green , and also by printed
posters displayedconspicuouslyin each of the voting
precinctsin Warren county. Said electionshall be
held in the same manner and by the same partiesnow
authorized to conduct a general electionin Warren
county;and ifa majorityof thosevotingshallvotein
favorof such subscription,then the county court shall
make the payments , and take the stock as herein pro-
vided. For the purpose of ascertainingthe willof
the people on said question, two columns shallbe
opened in each poll-book for each precinctin the
county at saidelection, one headed "For Turnpikes,"
and the other headed "Against Turnpikes ." . Before
such question is voted upon , said county court shall
cause notices of the fact of such submission to the
voters of Warren county for at least twenty days
before said election, such noticesto be published in
postersand weekly newspapers .
§ 8. If the county court deem it best, it may ,
insteadof paying the subscriptionherein authorized

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 519
inmoney,pay thesame inbondsofthecounty,tobe
issued in denominations of one hundred dollars and
fivehundred dollars, payable to bearer twenty years
afterdate, redeemable at any time afterfiveyears
from date, bearingfive(5)per cent.interestfrom date,
and payable semi-annually January 1 and July 1 of
each year, with coupons attached. Said bonds ,if so
issued,shallbe sold at not less than par, or paid to
thecompany in which the stock istaken at theirface
value. Said bonds or theirproceeds shall be used in

facethat they are in aid of turnpikes,and the clerk

bonds sold under this act, date of sale, number and

§9 . The interestof the county in any road, to the
extent of its stock in such road , shall at no time be
liablefor any debt or debts contractedby such com-
pan y .
§ 10. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 10,1886.
CHAPTER 1013 .
AN ACT to createand establisha new votingprecinctin the county
of Christian .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That thereishereby created and establisheda
new votingprecinctin the county of Christian, to be
known as Kelly's Station precinct; said precinctto
be composed of a part of the Hopkinsville Fruit
Hill, Crofton and Hamby districts, and bounded as
follows, to wit: Beginning at a point about (200)
two hundred yards east of the residenceof George
Browder; thencein a northwardlydirection,passing

520 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to the east of the residence of G. W. Clark and J. W.
Walker , and continuing to a point (200) two hundred
yards northeast of the residence of O. T. Fuller;
thence in a westwardly direction, leaving the resi-
dences of Geo . Grant and Webber Davis to the south,
and continuingto a point(200) two hundred yards
north-west of the residenceof Young Boyd ; thence in
a southerly direction,leaving the residencesof Wm .
Faulkner ,Thos .Fuller and Wm .R. Long to the east,
and continuing to a point (200) two hundred yards
south-west of the residenceof J. W. Sadler; thence in
a south-westerlydirection, leaving the residence of
James Miles to the north, and continuing to a point
three hundred (300) yards south of the residenceof
Henry Boyd ; thence in a north-westerly course to
the beginning. The voting place in said precinctis
hereby fixed at Kelly'sStation, untilotherwise fixed
by order of the Christiancounty court; and all per-
sons livingwithinsaidboundary ,who are legalvoters,
shall hereaftervote at said Kelly's Station, or at the
voting place in said precinctwhich may be hereafter
establishedby order of said court: Provided , how-
ever,that saidvoting place shallnot be changed be-
forethe August election, 1886.
§ 2. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved May 10, 1886.

CHAPTER 1023 .

county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

Spencer county shallbe four insteadof seven, as now

voting precincts to constitute one of said districts; the
Smileytown and Goodwin Spring voting precinctsto
constitute one of said districts; the Waterfield and
Elk Creek voting precincts to constitute one of said
districts; and the Taylorsvillevotingprecinctto con-
stitute one of said districts.
§2 . On the firstMonday inAugust ,1886,and there-
afteras providedby law, two justicesof the peace
and one constable shall be elected in each of said dis-
tricts; one of the courts to be held in the first-named
districtsshall be holden at or near Little Mount , and
one at or near Mount Eden . In the second named

In third -named district one court shall be held at or
near Waterfield, and one at or near the Elk Creek
post office;and in the fourth-named districtthe courts

passage .

CHAPTER 1039 .
AN ACT to prohibit the sale of spirituous, vinous and malt liquorsin
Breckinridge county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful forany person to sell,
barteror trafficin spirituous,vinous or malt liquors,
in any quantity whatever,and for any purpose what-
ever, either directlyor indirectly, in the county of
Breckinridge .
§ 2. Any person violating the provisions of the pre-
ceding sectionshall, upon conviction,be finedin any
sum not less than one hundred nor more than five
hundred dollars,in the discretionof the jury trying
the case .
§ 3. This act shall not take effector be in force until
the same shallbe ratifiedand approved by a majority
of the votes cast at the electionhereinafterprovided
for in this act .
§ 4. Said electionshallbe held at the regularelec-
tionon the firstTuesday in November , 1886, or atthe
regularAugust electionforcounty officesin 1887; this
act to not be construed so as to submit the vote more
than one time, but gives choiceof time so as tosuit
the peopleor votersof Breckinridgecounty; andit
shallbe unlawful for any person to vote at said elec-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 553
tionwho is not eligibleto vote forRepresentative to
- theGeneral Assembly . It shallbe the duty of the
clerkof the Breckinridge county court, in preparing
thepoll-books for said election,to make one column,
marked at the head thereof on each page, " For the
saleof spirituous, vinous and malt liquors," and
another column marked at the head of each page ,
"Against the sale of spirituous, vinous and malt
liquors."
§5. Itshallbe the duty of the clerkof said election
topropound to each individual who offers to vote at
saidelectionthe question, "Are you foror againstthe
saleof whisky in the county of Breckinridge?" and
his vote shall be recorded according to his answer .
The officersof the electionshallbe governed by the
lawsnow regulatingthe conduct of officersof general
elections. The poll-books shallbe returned,compared
and resultcertifiedas now requiredby law regulating
general elections. If it shall appear from a com-
parisonof the pollsthat a majority of the votescast
at saidelectionare againstthe sale of said liquors,
thenthe board to examine the pollsshallmake a cer-
tificateof that fact, which shallbe tiledwith the county
clerk,who shallrecordthe same in his office,and then
this act shall be in full force and effect.
§ 6. Itishereby made the duty of the Breckinridge
countycourtto order the sheriffto give twenty days'
noticeof saidelectionby printedor writtenpostersin
eachof the voting placesof saidcounty .
§7. Nothing in thisact shallbe construedto inter-
ferewith the privilegeof any person who is selling
liquorsunder a licenseproperlygranted untilthe
expiration of such license.
$ 8. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 12,1886 .

of a tax to keep saidroad in repair.

monwealth of Kentucky :

the Bradford and Lenoxburg Turnpike Road Com-
pany," approved the fourth day of March ,1880, be

the said turnpike company are authorized, by and
with the consent of the Bracken county court, and
alltheotherstockholdersof said company ,to remove
thetoll-gatesfrom,and open the saidBradford and
Lenoxburg Turnpike Road to the publictravel,free,
forthe space of fiveyears,and for such furthertime
asmay be agreed upon at the expirationof said five
years; and for the purpose of preserving said road
and keeping same in repair while it is open to the
publictravel free, the said directors are authorized to
levyan annual ad valorem tax, of not exceeding
fifteen cents on the one hundred dollars ' worth of
taxableproperty,which is situatedalong and within
three-quartersof a mile of the lineof saidturnpike

556 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
road,which shallbe in addition to the county road

same in repair.
§2 . The directorsof said turnpike road company
shallmeet on the firstSaturday in June each year,for

they shallmake the lastassessor'sreportof valuation
of said property for State revenue the basis of value
for taxation under this act. They shall make out a
listof the property to be taxed and assesssame,and
levy same by written resolution,signed by the presi-
dent and attested by the secretary of the board,

levy .

Bracken county are due and payable,and theymay
be worked out by the tax-payer in the same way and
manner ,and at the same time and pricesforlaborand
teams provided for in the county road law of said
county .

shallhave power to divide said road into severaldis-
tricts,not exceeding four in number ; and they shall
appointa surveyorfor each district,who shallhold
his officefortwo years, and untilhissuccessorisduly
appointed and qualified; and all work done on said
road shall be done under the order and directions of
said surveyor, in his respectivedistrict, and such
times as he may requireby notice and demand ; and
saidsurveyor shallcollectalltaxes not paid inwork
due on the property located in each districtrespec-
tively, after same has become due , and shallhave
such powers,rightsand privilegesin the collectionof
the taxesafterthey become delinquentas sheriffsnow
have forcollectingStaterevenue ; and said surveyors
shallreceivefor theirservices,as a compensation,an

and the same shallfirstbe appliedto the paymentof

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 557
his taxes due , and after he has exhausted his taxes
out ofany fund in his hands .

collectorof taxes for the whole of said road , instead of
thesurveyor,who shallhave likepowers as the sher-
iffto collectthe delinquent taxes, and may require
ofhim such bond as they deem best,and may have a
rightof actionthereon for allmoneys receivedby him .
- He shallhave a compensation of not exceeding twenty
per centum commission of taxes collected, and he shall

ingthe county road tax ; but such amount of tax as is

repairthisroad, may be continued and used in con-
junctionwith the money raisedunder thisact.
$8. There shallbe a penaltyof ten per centum added

afteritspassage.
Approved May 12,1886.

CHAPTER 1050 .
AN ACT to preventthe running at largeof horses,cows,sheep,hogs,
and otherdomesticanimalsin Christiancounty.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That from and afterthe firstday of March ,
1887,itshallnot be lawful forthe owner of any horse,
cow,sheep, hog or otherdomesticanimal to permit
thesame to run at largein Christiancounty .
§2. The owner of any suchdomesticanimalsoper-
mitting it to run at large shall be responsible for all
damage done by it to the farm or premises or other
propertyof any other person insaidcounty ; and when
any such damage isdone itshallbe lawful forthe per-
son sufferingthe same to take up such animal,and to
notifythe owner thereofwithin fivedays thereafter;
and ifwithin the next ten days from such noticethe
owner of such animal, or the person entitledto the
possessionthereof,shallpay to the owner of the prop

564 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
erty injuredallsuch damages and the costsof taking
up said animal, the same shall be returned to him .
But if for ten days after such notice is given the
owner or person entitledto the possessionof such ani-
mal shallfailor refuseto pay such damage and costs,
the person so taking up said animal may sellsame at
public auctionatthe nearestvillage,town or city,after
reasonable notice of the time and place of the sale,and
retainof the proceedsof the salesufficientto pay said
costsand damages ,and shallreturn the remainder,if
any, to the owner or person entitledto the possession
of such animal .
§ 3. This act shall take effect and become a law at
the time stated in the firstsectionof this act, upon
the followingconditions: A poll shallbe opened at
each voting precinct or place in the county , at the
congressionalelection in November , 1886, and the
questionshallbe put by the officersof the electionto
each person offeringto vote at said election,Are you
in favor of the stock law ? and shall record his vote
foror againstit, accordingto his answer. The result
of such electionshallbe certifiedand compared by the
same officers and at the same time that the result of
the congressionalelectionis certifiedand compared ,
and if it shallappear, from such comparison, to the
county judge that a majority of persons voting in
said election and on said question have voted in
favorof said law ,itshallbe the duty of the judge to
enter the fact on the order book of the court, and
to give noticethereofin the newspapers published in
the county during that or the followingmonth .
§ 4. In taking the vote the officersof election, in
such precinctsor votingdistrictsas aredivided by the
line herein below designated, shallnote, by writing
the letterN or S after the name of each voter, whether
he livesnorth or south of said line; and if, upon a
comparison of the poll-books, itshallappear that the
majority of the persons voting in either division of
the county made by said linehave voted against the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 565
law,itshallnot take effectand be in forcein that por-
tion of the county, although a majority of all the
votescast in the county may have been in favor of it.
But it shall take effect and be in force in the other
portionof the county in the manner and at the time
statedin section3 of this act, provided a majority
therein vote for it.
§5 . The line of division referred to above shall
beginon the east sideof the county,where the But-
ler road intersectsthe county line, and shall run
thence with the Butler road in a westward course
till said road intersects the Russellville road near
Hopkinsville; thence with the last named road in
the same direction until it reaches the limits of the
cityof Hopkinsville; thence with the boundary line
of said city and on the southern side thereof until
it reaches the Princeton road on the western side of
thecity; thence with saidPrinceton road to the point
on saidroad where itintersectsthe county line.
Approved May 13,1886.

CHAPTER 1086 .
AN ACT to authorize the town of Danville to raisemoney to aid in
the construction of railroads.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the town of Dan-
ville, in Boyle county, of this State, to issue bonds

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 617
on the creditand responsibilityof the saidtown and

sums not exceeding fiftythousand dollars, in order
to raisemoney to aid in the constructionof one or
more railroadsto saidtown , or one or more railroads
on which said town shall be made a permanent sta-
tion .
§ 2. That before any bonds shallbe issued for said
purpose , an electionshallbe held in saidtown at the
usual voting place or placesfor the electionof trus-
tees of said town or such like town authorities, at
which the sense of the qualifiedvoters of said town
shall be taken for and against the proposition to
issue bonds as aforesaidfor railroadpurposes ; said
elections shall be held in the same manner , as near as
may be, as the electionof trusteesof said town are
held by law in said town , and under the same safe-
guards , responsibilitiesand penaltiesas respectsoffi-
cers and voters as apply to other electionsin said
town ; and the same method of ascertainingand certi-
fying the resultshallapply as in such elections. If
the proposition shallreceivea majority of qualified
voters of said town voting at such election, the same
shall be entered of record on the minute books of
the trusteesof said town , and such entry shall be
sufficient evidence of the result as well as of the
validity of the proceedings touching the same :
Provided , The board of trustees of the town of Dan-
villeshallhave power to determine allquestions per-
taining to such election before said order is made .
Before such electionshallbe held, any railroadcorpo-
ration desiring to avail itself of the benefit of this
act shall, through its president or other authorized
officers, apply in writing to the said board of trustees
of said town ,proposing to exchange itsbonds orstock
at a rateto be named in said applicationfor the bonds
of said town , to an amount equal to the whole or a
part of saidsum of fiftythousand dollars; and upon
such application the said board of trustees and a com

618 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
mittee of the citizensof said town , consistingof Jno .
W. Procter, Geo . W. Welsh ,Jr., Jno .M. Meyer and
Jno . S. Van Winkle , shall determine whether said
election shall be held , and what sum of bonds of said
town shallbe included in such proposition; and if
said board and committee determine to submit a prop-
osition to the voters of the town , an election shall be
ordered (such order being entered on the record book
of the board of trustees), and of which election not
lessthan twenty days' notice, signed by the chairman
of said board of trustees and three of said committee
of citizens,shallbe given by the publicationin one or
more newspapers published in said town . Elections
may be held under this act until the whole amount of
fiftythousand dollarsshallbe authorized, at the dis-
cretion of said board of trustees and said committee of
citizens. If any one or more of said committee shall
die,declineto act,remove ,or become disabled,the re-
maining members shallhave power to fillallvacancies.
§3 . The bonds authorized by this act shall bear not
exceeding six per cent.annual interest,payable semi-
annually or annually, as said board of trustees may
determine ; and saidbonds and coupons shall be pay-
able at such times and at such place as said board
may direct; and said bonds shallnot be disposed of
at lessthan par value. The bonds shallbe signed by
the chairman of said board of trustees or mayor of
said town , as the case may be, and countersigned by
the clerk or secretary of said board or town council ;
and the coupons shallalsobe signed by such chairman
or mayor . A record shallbe kept by the authorities
of said town as to the issuance of said bonds , and the
chairman of said board , mayor and other officers of
said town, who may be concerned in the issuance of
saidbonds or thecustodythereof,shallbe responsi-
ble, officially, on their respectivebonds , if any , and
personallyforthe safe-keeping and lawful disposition
of all bonds which may be issued under this act.
§ 4. The board of trusteesof saidtown , or such mu-

nicipalbody or bodies that may succeed them ,shall
havepower to levy a tax upon the property of the cit-
izensof saidtown , and allproperty locatedwithin the

nicipalbonds , and shall safelykeep or deliver the

CHAPTER 1087 .

a specialschool tax for the purposes of tuition,and to authorizethe
trusteesto extend school term in such districtsbeyond a fivemonths '
term .
WHEREAS , Certain school districtsin this State have

eralschool law failsto provide any authority for

620 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

sufficientmoney to defray the expenses of a ten
months ' school; therefore,

monwealth of Kentucky :

when the people have voted on themselves a tax for
school purposes and collectedthe same since the pas-

common schoolsin thisCommonwealth ," approved on

year .

§ 3. That this act shall be in force and take effect
from and afteritspassage.

CHAPTER 1093 .

Henry county.

tribute the proceeds of said sale, afterthe payment
of the costsof such proceedings, between E. F. Ditto,

owners of the stock in said road, according to their
rights. The purchaser is hereby invested with all
40--Local Laws , vol.2.

the rights, privilegesand franchisesnow belonging

lehem Turnpike Road Company .

saleof the Pleasureville, Bethlehem and Kentucky
River Turnpike Road , the court may , if it deem it
proper, order the sale of the Bethlehem Turnpike
Road to be made in connection with the sale of the

CHAPTER 1094 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheNationalSickand AccidentFund inAdair-
ville, Logan county, Kentucky .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Dr. R. P. Townsend ,E. Fasy , M. L. Fu-
gate, W. E. Jenkins, J. W. Porter, F. L. McKernan ,
J. G. Orndorff and S. C. Gooch , and their associates
and successors,be,and are hereby, createdthe body-
politic, under the name of the National Sick and
Accident Fund , with power to sue and be sued, con-
tractand be contractedwith, and shallhave perpet-
ual succession,with rights to hold real estate and
other property and funds necessary, in the judgment
of the board of directory,for itsuse and purposes,
and may disposeof the same at will; and may adopt
and use a common seal,renewable and changeable at

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 627
pleasure;and may make such by -laws and regulations
as may be deemed necessary to fully carry out the
purposes of this act of incorporation .
§2. The principalplace of businessof saidcorpora-
tionshallbe Adairville, Kentucky ; and any four of
the incorporatorsabove named may , in person or by
proxy, afterbeing duly notified, proceed to organize
and electboard of directoryand other officerspro-
vided herein .
§3 .The affairsof saidcorporationshallbe managed
by a board of directoryof not lessthan fivenor more
than fifteenpersons; seven of saidboard shallbe resi-
dentsofLogan county,Kentucky,and shallconstitute
a localboard , a majority of which shall constitutea
quorum to do business; the remaining eight shall
constitutean advisory board, and may residein any
partof the Stateof Kentucky . The board of directory
shallbe chosen by the members of the corporationon
the fourth Tuesday in June of each year, and shall
enterupon the discharge of theirduties on the first
day of July thereafter. They may be qualified
at any time after their election; and previous to
entering upon the discharge of their duties the
presidentof said corporation, or any person com-
petent to administer an oath, may qualify said
members elect. Board of directory and all other
officersshall hold their offices until their successors
are elected and installed . Vacancies in the board
may be filledby the board itselfand any directorso
elected shall hold his office until the next annual elec-
tion. Should the firstof July fallon Sunday, the
directorselectshallmeet on the day precedingand
organize. Should no electionbe held on the day men-
tionedabove, the president, by orderof the board,
may calla meeting for such purpose upon ten days '
notice,published in some newspaper in Kentucky or
otherwise .
§4. The board shallelectfrom itsnumber a presi-
dent, a vice-president, a secretaryand a treasurer,

628 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and such other officersas it may be necessary to man-
age the businessof the corporation,and may regulate
the dutiesof each by by -laws.
§5 . The objectof the corporationshallbe to pro-
vide weekly benefitsfor itsmembers during sickness
orwhen disabledby accident; and theboard shallhave
power to assessitsmembers to pay such benefit, also
to charge a membership fee and quarterly dues , the
amount of all of which it may fix by by -laws . It
shallhave power to prescribeby itsby -laws the con-
ditionsupon which members shall be admitted, and
classifythe risks, and fixthe assessments and mem
bership fee in accordance therewith; to divide the
risksinto separate classesif they think best, and
keep the business of each class separate from the
other .
§ 6. The weekly benefitsshallnot be lessthan five
nor more than fiftydollars,to be paid to the applicant
upon conditions prescribed by the by -laws of said
board : Provided ,however,That no more than twenty-
six weeks shallbe paid to any member within any one
year .
§7. No person shallbe admitted a member of said
corporationuntilhe shallhave complied with the con-
ditionsprescribedby the by -laws of said corporation.
The board shall have power to appoint a medical
examiner, who shall be a graduate of some medical
college, to whom allapplicationfor membership shall
be submitted, and he shallwrite his opinion on said
application; and upon the approval of said medical
examiner and the payment of such fees, assessments
and so forth, as the by -laws may prescribe, shallbe-
come a member of said corporation, unlessthe board
directotherwise, and a certificateof membership shall
be issued to him ,signedby thepresidentand secretary
of the board .
§8.No personshallbe eligibleto membershipin
said corporationexcept free-born white men between
the ages of eighteenand forty-five.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 629
§ 9. The board shallcreatetwo separatefunds, one
to be known as the " benefit fund " and the other as
the " permanent fund ." Ninety per cent. of allmon-
eys received from assessments shall be placed to the
creditof the " benefitfund ," and shallbe used only to
pay weekly benefits. All moneys receivedfrom fees,
dues and other sources, and the remaining ten per
cent.of assessments,shallbe placedto the creditof the
permanent fund,and may be used to defray the ex-
penses of the corporation,or as the board may direct.
§ 10. The weekly benefitdue members shallnot be
subject to the claims of creditors, and shallnot be
reached by attachment, garnishment, or other pro-
cessesof law, so as to divertit from itsmembers or
their families .
§ 11. The members of the corporationshallbe bound
inlaw to pay allassessments,dues and so forth; they
may , however , withdraw from the corporationby giv-
ing written notice to the secretaryor chief officerof
the corporationof such intention, afterwhich alllia-
bilitiesshall cease for assessments , dues and so forth,
that may thereafteraccrue; and in case of failureto
pay assessments, quarterly dues and so forth, shall
be debarred from receiving any benefitswhatever
to which they might otherwise have become entitled
during such delinquency. The board may suspend
any member forfailureto pay dues, assessments and
so forth, and when so suspended can only be restored
to membership by actionof the board.
§ 12. All claims againstthe corporationforbenefits
must be assertedwithin one year from the time when
they might have been claimed, or the rightto recover
same shall be forfeited .
§ 13. The individualshallin no way be liableforthe
debts of the corporation .
§ 14. This associationshallbe subjectto the act of
April 19, 1884,regulatingco-operativeinsurance com-
panies of other States,so far as the same may be appli-
cable to a company of thisState.

630 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 15. All benefitclaimsshallbe paid as soon as prac-
ticableafterthe necessaryproof has been made ; but in
no case shallpayment be withheld more than sixty
days aftertheproofhas been approvedby theboard.
§ 16. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 14, 1886.

Turnpike Road Company , for the purpose of build-

five dollars each .

CHAPTER 1120 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe sale of vinous, spirituousand malt liquors
in Ohio county .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person or
personsto sell,barter or trafficin spirituous,vinous
or malt liquors, in any quantity whatever, either
directlyor indirectly, in the county of Ohio .
§2 .Any person violatingthe provisionsof the pre-
cedingsectionshall,upon conviction,be finedin any
sum not less than one hundred nor more than five
hundreddollars,in thediscretionof thejurytrying
the case .
§ 3. This act shall not take effector be in force until
the same shallbe ratifiedand approved by a majority
of the votes cast at the electionhereinafterprovided
for in this act .
§ 4. Said electionshallbe held at the regular elec-
tionon Tuesday afterthe firstMonday in November ,
1886; and itshallbe unlawful for any person to vote
at said electionwho is not eligibleto vote for Repre-
sentativeto the General Assembly . It shallbe the
duty of the clerk of the Ohio county court,in pre-
paring the poll-books for said election,to make one
column , marked at the head thereof on each page ,
" For the saleof spirituous, vinous and malt liquors,"
and another column marked at the head of each page,
"Against the sale of spirituous, vinous and malt
liquors ."
§5 . It shall be the duty of the clerk, judge or
sheriff of said electionto propound to each indi-
vidual who offers to vote at said election the ques-
tion, " Are you for or against the sale of whisky in
the county of Ohio ?" and his vote shallbe recorded
according to his answer. The officersof electionshall
be governed by the lawsnow regulatingthe conduct

656 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ofgeneralelections. The poll-booksshallbereturned. (
compared and resultcertifiedas now required by law
regulatinggeneralelections. If itshallappear from a
comparison of the pollsthat a majority of the votes
castatsaidelectionareagainstthe saleof said liquors,
then the board to examine the polls shall make a
certificateof that fact,which shallbe filedwith the
county clerk, who shallrecord the same in his office,
and then this act shall be in full force and effect.
§ 6. It is hereby made the duty of the Ohio county
court to order the sheriffof said county to give at
leasttwenty days' noticeof the time of said election.
by written or printed noticesposted in each of the
votingplacesof saidcounty.
§ 7. Nothing in this act shall be construed to inter-
ferewith the privilegeof any person sellingliquors,
properly granted,tillthe expirationof said license.
§ 8. This act,in so faras itauthorizesthe vote tobe
taken,shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
§ 9. All acts and parts of acts inconsistenthere-
with are hereby repealed .
Approved May 15, 1886.

CHAPTER 1124 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Adam Rueling .
WHEREAS , Adam Rueling was , on the twenty-first
day of September , 1885, granted, by the Jefferson
county court,licenseto run a tavern in Springdale
precinct, for which he paid to the clerk of said court
for State license the sum of one hundred dollars
($ 100); and whereas, by a decisionof the Court of
Appeals rendered on the tenth day of December ,
1885, the saleof liquor in said precinctwas made un-
lawful, and by said decisionhis licensebecame void ;
therefore ,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be, and
he is hereby , directedto draw his warrant on the
Treasurer in favor of Adam Rueling for the sum of
seventy-fivedollarsand ninety-nine cents($75.99).
§ 2. This actto be in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved May 14,1886.

ing of thisact; and of such policiesor contracts, and
allsuch policiesand contracts so made , subscribed,

monwealth of Kentucky :

of Lexington are hereby authorized and empowered
to issuebonds of saidcityin any amount not exceed-

from, at the option of the city, and bearing interestat

of said bonds to be applied to the purchase of a suit-
able lot or lots, and the erection thereon of a school-

annual revenue of the city,as a sinking fund , out of

sage .

CHAPTER 1162 .

and Camdenville Turnpike , at or near the residence

Catlettand R. C. Littlepage. The capitalstock of

company , in the name of Anderson county, to an

mile in length of said road, and shallbe governed in
making saidsubscriptionsand levyingand collecting
the tax to pay the same by the provisionsof an act
authorizingthe county of Anderson to take and hold
stock in certainroads in said county, approved March
10,1854 ; and saidact, so far as the same isapplicable

hereof .
§ 3. That sections2,3,4,5,6,7, 8,9, 11, 12,13 and
14 of an act,entitled "An act to incorporate the Cam-
denville, Johnsonville and Burnt Church Turnpike
Road Company ," approved February 24,1870, as far

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 713
as they are applicableto and not inconsistentwith
the provisions of this act,are made and enacted as
part hereof .
§4 . That when said road is completed they shall
have power to establisha toll-gate, and shall tollat
the rate now provided by law in that proportion per
mile as provided by law ; and no law heretoforeen-
actedshallprecludethe county court from taking
stock in said road on account of the length of said
road being lessthan fivemiles .
§5 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 15, 1886.

CHAPTER 1166 .

to or near Old Long Run Church ; and thencewith
the course of the centralbranch of said creek,de-

Colby Cowherd , Stephen Lowry , Andrew Campbell ,

open books and receive subscriptionsto the capital

they may choose. The capital stock shall be ten
thousand dollars,and dividedintosharesoffiftydol-
lars,and the amount of the capitalstockmay , from

thousand dollars. The corporate indebtednessshall
at no time exceed two-thirds of the capitalstock ;

berpresident,and alsoappointa secretaryand treas-
urerfrom among the stockholders. A majority of the
directorsshallbe a quorum forthetransactionof any
businessof thecompany ; and saidboard of directors
shallcontrolthe affairsof the company .
§5. The principalplaceof businessshallbe at or
near Long Run Church ; and itselectionsand other
meetingsshallbe heldon noticegivenby theboard of
directors; and the votingshallbe in'person or by
:

716 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
written proxy , and each share of stock shallbe en-
titled to one vote .

CHAPTER 1169 .
AN ACT forthe continuationof the GeologicalSurvey and fixingthe
salaryof the State Geologist.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. That for the continuationof the Geological,
Topographicaland AgriculturalSurvey of the State,
the chemical analysisof soilsand other substances,
thecollectingof and testingof coals,iron ores,build-
ing stones,clays and othersubstances,thereishereby
appropriated,from any money in the State Treasury
not otherwise appropriated, the sum of ten thousand
dollarsper annum , to be expended in the same way
and manner as provided in chapter877 of the Session
Acts of 1883 and 1884: Provided, That the State
Geologist shall make an itemized statement of all
expendituresmade by him to each sessionof the Gen-
eralAssembly . Before any of the money herein ap-
propriatedispaid to the State Geologist,or upon his
order, said Geologist shall execute bond in the sum
of twenty thousand dollarsto the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , and good and sufficientsurety, to be ap-
proved by the Governor, for the lawful discharge of

720 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
hisduties, which bond shallbe attestedby the Secre-
tary of State and filedin his office.
§ 2. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afteritspassage; but the foregoingappropriation
shall only be for two years : Provided , That all ex-
penses of printing and lithographing, and all other
expense connected with the GeologicalSurvey , shall
be paid out of this appropriation: But provided
further, That all moneys received from the sale of
publicationsof the bureau shall be placed by the
Auditor to the creditof the GeologicalSurvey .

and afterthe date of the passage of this act, shall
be two thousand dollars .
Approved May 15,1886.

tion .

provided, be applicableto and regulatethe common

uary, 1887 : Provided, That between the passageof

ed or provided of sufficientcapacity to hold allthe

CHAPTER 1175 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Consumers' Gas-lightCompany of Cov-
ington.

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That L. E. Casey ,F. P. Helm , F. G. Oxley , H.
B. Wells and Alexander Davezac,and theirassociates,
successors and assigns, be, and they are hereby,in-
corporated and created a body -politic,by the name of

with, including the right to purchase,hold, selland
convey real estate; to have and use a common seal,
and change and alterthe same at pleasure, and gener-
allyto do and perform allthingsnecessaryto be done
and performed in the conduct of itsbusinessherein-
afterset forth; to ordain and put in execution such
by -laws ,rules and regulationsfor the government of
itsaffairsas may be deemed expedientand proper;
and the same may be amended or alteredat any reg-
ular or calledmeeting by a majorityof the stock,after

regularor calledmeeting held notlessthan thirtydays

and laws of this State or of the United States .

thousand dollars, and shall be divided into shares

than fivedollarseach,in the discretionof the corpo-

nation and part of other denominations . The stock
