a lienupon thesharesof any stockholderwho may be

724 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
indebted to it; and such shares shall not be transfera-
ble without the consent of the presidentand directors
firsthad , or untilsaiddebt shallhave been firstpaid
and discharged .
§3 . The corporation shallbe governed by a board
of fivedirectors, which number may be increasedto
not exceeding nine. The directorsshall be elected
annually by the stockholdersfrom theirnumber , on
the firstMonday in January of each year, and shall
hold theirofficeuntiltheir successorsare duly elected
and qualified. The directors shall elect from their
number a presidentand vice-president,and may elect
a secretaryand treasurer,who shallbe a stockholder
in said company , and may appoint such other officers
and agents as may be necessaryforthe proper conduct
of the business and affairsof the company ,and may
fixtheir compensation, and may receivebond of any
or all of them for the faithfulperformance of their

pany ,whether in one or more shares, shallentitlethe
holder or holders thereofto one vote,in person or by

§ 5. Said corporationishereby authorized to create
an indebtedness in such amount as the stockholders
may order, and may issue itsbonds therefor, secured
by lien or mortgage upon its property, rights and
franchises .
§ 6. The business of said company is limited to
manufacturing,furnishingand supplying the cityof
Covington and itsinhabitants, and persons and cor-
porationslocatedin or near saidcity,with coal,water,
or othergas for lighting, heating and other purposes,
and may charge therefor such price and compensation
as may be agreed upon between saidcompany and the
individualsor corporationscontractingwith it. It

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 725
may supply such gas to itsstockholdersalone, ifit
shall so desire, at cost, or at such price as may be
decided upon by itsboard of directors,or may so fur-
nish and supply the same to eitheror all.
§ 7. Said corporationshallhave power and author-
ity, subject to the same regulationsand restrictions

mains and conduitsunder the streets, alleysand high-

ing itspipes,mains and conduits under or upon said
streets, alleysor highways ,firstobtain from the city
councilof saidcityof Covington permission so to do .
§ 9. A non -user of this charter for five years shall
work a forfeiture thereof .
§ 10. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 15,1886 .

CHAPTER 1178 .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. L. Edmunds , F. W. Darby , H. F.

by , created a body -politicand corporate, under the
name of the Clarksville and Ohio River Railroad
Company ; and by thatname shallhave the power to
contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued,

construct and operate a railroadfrom any point on
the line between the States of Tennessee and Ken-
tucky , in Christianor Trigg county,by way of Prince-
ton, in Caldwell county, to any point on the Ohio
river above Carrsville and below the mouth of Green
river .
§2 . The business of said company shall be con-
ducted under the immediate direction of a board of
directors, consistingof seven members , to be selected
by the stockholdersof said company from among
themselves ; and at leastfour of said directorsshall
be citizens of Kentucky ; and said board shallelect
one of their number to be presidentof the board,and
such person so selectedshallbe presidentof the said
railroad company .

thousand dollarsof bona fide subscriptionsof stock

construction and maintenance of the railroad and its

urer. Theseofficersshallbe selectedby theboardof

CHAPTER 1185 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof J. C. Smock ,of Mercer county .
WHEREAS , J. C. Smock , deputy sheriffof Mercer
county,was , June 20, 1885,named as agent in a requi-
sitionissued by the Governor of Kentucky upon the
Governor of Texas for the person of J. B. Davidson ,
indictedfor felonyin the Mercer circuitcourtand a
fugitivefrom justicein the State of Texas ; that said
Smock , as such agent,paid the Secretaryof State of
Kentucky the sum of nine dollarsas fees for requi-
sition; that he paid the Secretaryof State of Texas
the sum of four dollars for warrant of arrest; that he
arrested said Davidson , and while en route with the
prisoner,Davidson ,a writ of habeas corpus was served
upon him ,issuedby the proper authoritiesof Abilene,
Texas, to show cause why he detained said prisoner,
Davidson ; that upon a trialhe hired counseland paid
officers' feesto the amount of fiftydollars; that he was
successfulin his resistanceof said action, but that the
Governor of Texas revoked his order and he was com-
pelledto return without the fugitive, Davidson ; that
hisexpenses were two dollarsper day forthirty-three
days,amounting to sixty-sixdollars; thathe isjustly
entitledto one hundred and ninety-fivedollarsas mile-
agefortravelingthreethousand two hundred and fifty
miles,atsix centsper mile; thatthe aggregateamount

748 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of hisexpenditures,mileageand compensation,isthree
hundred and twenty -four dollars; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be direct-
ed to draw his warrant upon the Treasurer of the State
forthe sum of three hundred and twenty fourdollars
in favor of J. C. Smock .
§ 2. This actto take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 15,1886.

stockinturnpikeroadsinsaidcounty.

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Allen county be,and
itis hereby , empowered to pay to any turnpike com-
pany which has been or may hereafterbe incorporated

shallhave completed one mile of well-constructedand

shallhave given,in writing,a statementshowing that
saidcompany has ample means or privatesubscription
on hand with which ,and the one thousand dollarsper
mile to be drawn from the county,to constructand
put in operation at leastthree miles of turnpike road .

cause said road to be carefully examined .

fortheamount so paid by her,and saidcounty,act-
ingthroughthecountycourt,shallparticipateinthe
controland management , and share in the dividends
ofany such roadas any otherstockholder.

to be built; but in taking such stock shall be as

ble; but in no event shallsaid county court pay to-
wards ,or saidcounty be a stockholderin,the roads of
said county beyond the sum of twenty thousand dol-
lars .

by said county shallbe kept of record in the county
court clerk'soffice, and the county court clerk shall
keep a book in which shall be recorded the date and
amount of each subscriptionof stockby the county of
Allen, in what company ,and when said subscription

on the stock held by her in any turnpike company

ty named .

ticesto be published in posters.

money , pay the same in bonds of the county, to be

CHAPTER 1194 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof E. H. Mottley,of Warren county.
WHEREAS , At a late term of the Warren circuit
courtone F. M. Ramsey , who was at that time acting
as specialbailiffin the employ of R. H. Crittenden,
United States Marshal for the State of Kentucky ,
was indictedby the grand jury of Warren county,
charging him therein with the murder of one Joseph
Lightfoot in Warren county ; and whereas, a bench
warrant,by order of the court,was issued and placed
in the hands of E. H. Mottley, the then sheriffof
Warren county, and was by him executed by arresting
saidRamsey ; and whereas,whilesaidRamsey was in
confinement under said arrest and bench warrant , the
Hon .Bland Ballard, judge of the circuitcourt of the
United Statesfor the districtof Kentucky , upon the
petitionof said Ramsey , caused a writ of habeas
corpus to issue,upon which the body of saidRamsey
was taken from the said E. H. Mottley,sheriff, and
carriedbefore the United StatesCourt, and was by
saidcourt releasedfrom custody, Ramsey claiming
that inasmuch as he was an officer of said United
StatesCourt,and was actingwithin the lineand scope
of his duties as United States bailiff when he killed
Lightfoot, thathe was only amenableto the United
StatesCourt, and that a State court had no juris-
dictionof him . Said Ramsey being released from
custody by said court, came again into Warren
county, and the judge of the Warren circuitcourt
ordered another bench warrant to issue upon said

758 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
indictment, which bench warrant was placed inthe
hands of the said E. H. Mottley, and was by his
executed by again arrestingsaid Ramsey , and sait
Ramsey ,again making his petitionto the saidUnited
States Court, was by that court again releasedfrom
custody, and the costs in the said United States
Court, amounting to the sum of one hundred and
eighty-sixdollarsand sixty-eightcents, was adjudged
against the said sheriffof Warren , the said E. H.
Mottley personally,and executionwas issuedthereon
for the above sum against said Mottley personally.
now , therefore,
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of theCom
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts for the
State of Kentucky be, and he ishereby , directed,out
of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appro-
priated, to draw his warrant upon the Treasurerof
the Stateforthe sum of one hundred and eighty-six
dollarsand sixty-eightcents for the benefitof E. H.
Mottley , late sheriffof Warren county .
§2 . That this act shall take effectfrom and afterits
passage .
Approved May 17,1886.

CHAPTER 1197 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Mrs. E. E. Bone, of Hickman county.
WHEREAS ,F. O. Bone,ofHickman county,has,by
the circuitcourt of said county, been adjudged an
idiotwithoutestatefor hissupport,etc.,and placed
inthechargeof Mrs. E. E. Bone, hiscommittee, and
an allowanceof seventy-fivedollarsper annum to be
paidher out of the StateTreasury for the support of
saididiot. The fiveyears from the time of holding
thelastinquestexpiredat the March term, 1885,of
theHickman circuitcourt,and saidcommitteehaving

760 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
failedto have a new inquestheld, the Auditor ofPub-
licAccounts refused to pay the allowance of thirty-
seven dollarsand fiftycents made at the September
term, 1885, of said court, and on the fourth day of
February ,1886,said idiotdeparted this life, and the
said E. E. Bone ,committee,having contracteddebts
for the support and burial of said idiotfor as much
or more than the seventy-fivedollarsper annum ,with
the expectationof receivingsaid allowance from the
third Monday in March , 1885, to the fourth day of
February , 1886 :
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of theCom
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts draw his
warrant on the State Treasury in favor of Mrs. E. Ε.
Bone for the sum of sixty-two dollars and fiftycents.
being at the rate of seventy-fivedollars per annum
from the date of the lastpayment to the date of the
death of said idiot, out of any money in the State
Treasury not otherwiseappropriated.
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 17,1886.
CHAPTER 1198 .
AN ACT for the benefitof James H. Young ,of Adair county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he
ishereby , directed to draw his warrant on the Treas-
urer in favor of James H. Young for one hundred
dollars .
§ 2. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 17,1886.

CHAPTER 1230 .
AN ACT to punishand make seductiona felonyin thisCommonwealth .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That whoever shall,under promise of marriage,
seduce and have carnal knowledge of any female un-
der sixteenyears of age,shall be guiltyof a felony,
and,upon convictionthereof,shallbe confinedin the
penitentiarynot lessthan one year nor more than five
years ; and in all prosecutions under this act the
defendant shallhave the right to testifyin his own
behalf: Provided ,however, That no prosecutionsshall
be instituted when the person charged shall have mar-
riedthe girlseduced : And provided further, That
any prosecution instituted under this act shall be dis-
continuedif the party accused marry the girlseduced
beforefinaljudgment .
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of the severalcircuitjudges
and judges of the criminal courts in this Common-
wealth to give thisact in charge to the grand juriesof
theirrespectivecourts. All prosecutions under this
act shall be instituted within twelve months after the
commission of the offense .
§ 3. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved May 17, 1886.

CHAPTER 1240 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous,vinousand malt liquorsin
the county of Muhlenberg .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons to sell,barter or trafficin spirituous, vinous
or malt liquors,in any quantitywhatever,and for any
purpose whatever, eitherdirectlyor indirectly, in the
county of Muhlenberg .
§ 2. Any person violatingthe provisionsof the pre-
ceding sectionshall,upon conviction, be finedin any
sum not less than one hundred nor more than five
hundred dollars, in the discretionof the jury try
ing the case.
§3 . This act shall not take effector be in force
until the same shall be ratifiedand approved by a
majorityof the votescastatthe electionhereinafter
provided for in this act.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 825
§ 4. Said election shall be held on the firstSatur-
day in October, 1886, and it shallbe unlawful for
any persontovoteat saidelectionwho isnoteligible
to vote for Representative to the General Assembly .
It shallbe the duty of the clerk of the Muhlenberg
county court, in preparing the poll-books for said
election, to make one column marked at the head
thereof on each page , " For the sale of spirituous, vin-
ous and malt liquors," and another column marked
at the head of each page , "Against the sale of spirit-
uous , vinous and malt liquors."
§ 5. It shallbe the duty of the clerk of said elec-
tion to propound to each individualwho offersto
vote at said electionthe question, "Are you for or
againstthe sale of whisky in the county of Muhlen-
berg ?" and hisvote shallbe recordedaccordingto his
answer. The officersof saidelectionshallbe governed
by the laws now regulating the conduct of officers
of generalelection. The poll-books shallbe returned,
compared and resultcertifiedas now required by law
regulatinggeneralelections. If it shall appear from
a comparison of the pollsthat a majorityof the votes
castat saidelectionare againstthe said liquors,then
the board to examine the poll shallmake a certificate
of that fact, which shall be filedwith the county
clerk,who shallrecordthe same in his office,and then
this act shall be in full force and effect.
§ 6. Itishereby made the duty of the Muhlenberg
countycourttoorderthe sherifftogivetwenty days'
noticeof the saidelectionby written or printed post-
ersin each of the votingplacesof saidcounty.
§ 7. Nothing in this act shallbe construed to in-
terferewith the privilegeof persons who are selling
liquorsunder a licenseproperly granted tillthe expi-
ration of such license .
§ 8. This act,in so faras itauthorizesthe vote to be
taken, shall take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved May 17, 1886.

CHAPTER 1256 .
AN ACT todefinethe responsibilityof insurance companies for the acts
of theiragents.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That whoever solicitsinsurance on behalf of
any fireor lifeinsurance company ,or transmitsfora
person other than himself an applicationfor or a
policy of insurance to or from such company , or
advertises that he will receive or transmit the same ,
shall,anything in the applicationor policy to the con-
trarynotwithstanding, be held to be an agent of such
company to allintentsand purposes,unlessitappears
that he receives no commission or other compensation
or considerationfor such servicesfrom such company
or itsaccreditedagents,or thathe isthe duly author-
izedagent of the person in whose behalfsuch applica-
tionor policyismade or issued.
§ 2. That this act shall take effect and be in force
from and after itspassage .
Approved May 17,1886.

CHAPTER 1278 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof James Williams, of Cumberland county.
WHEREAS , At the April term, 1879,of the Cumber-
landcounty criminalcourt, James Williamswas fined
twenty-fivedollars and sentenced to imprisonment
ten days in the Cumberland county jailfor carrying
a concealed weapon ; and whereas, his Excellency
James B. McCreary, Governor of Kentucky, did,on
the fourteenthday of June, 1879, pardon saidWil-
liamsand remit the fineof twenty-fivedollars, less
the cost and commission; and whereas, said Wil-
liams,withoutany knowledge of saidpardon and re-
mission,paidoffand satisfiedthesaidfine; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be di

878 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
rected to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor
of James Williams for seventeen dollarsand fifty
cents,out of any money in the Treasury not other-
wise appropriated .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 18,1886.

CHAPTER 1282 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the city of Maysville.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled "An act to amend the
charterof the cityof Maysville," approved April 22,
1886,be, and the same ishereby, repealed.
§2 . That in lieu of said act it is now enacted :
First. That thereshallbe for the cityof Maysville
an officerto be styledthe cityprosecutor,who shall
be appointed annually by the mayor ; but such ap-
pointment shallbe subject to approval by the board
of councilmen of said city.
Second. That the city prosecutor shall hold his
officefor the term of one year , or until his succes-
sorshallhave duly qualified, unless sooner removed
by the board of councilmen in the exercise of the
righthereby granted it to remove him for lawful
cause .
Third . That the cityprosecutorshallappear at the
trialof and prosecute alloffendersagainstthe laws
and ordinancesof the cityof Maysville.
Fourth . That the city prosecutor shallreceivefor
such servicesa just compensation, which shallbe a
salarynot to exceed one hundred and fiftydollars
per annum , to be paid out of the city levy, and in
additiontheretoa sum equivalentto twenty per cent.
of the amount of the fine assessed in cases in which

886 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
he has personally appeared and prosecuted, which
said sum shallbe taxed as costsagainstthe defend-
ant therein .
Fifth . That no one shallbe eligibleto the officeof
city prosecutor who has not been two years a prac-
ticingattorneyand a residentcitizenof Maysville.
Sixth . That this act shall take effect from and after
itspassage .
Approved May 18, 1886 .

CHAPTER 1289 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled "An act to authorize the Trimble
county court to take stock in turnpike roads in said county."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled"An act to authorizethe
votersof Trimble county, Kentucky , to vote a tax for
turnpikepurposes," approved May 5, 1880, and all
amendments thereto, be amended as follows : That
allthe turnpikes hereafterbuilded under the law to
which this is an amendment shall be built of metal
twelvefeet wide and ten inches deep, six inches of
metal to be so macadamized or beaten up that the

894 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
rock willpass through a five-inch ring, to be covered
with gravelfour inchesdeep,or with stonefourinches
deep , so macadamized or beaten up that itwillpass
through a two and one-half inch ring,the whole to be
put upon a good road-bed properlygraded so asto not
exceed five degrees . The sides of the metal to be
shouldered two feet wide on each side, and ditched so
as to turn the water off of the turnpike; and when-
ever wooden bridges or culvertsare used in the con-
structionof turnpikes, they are to be made with hewn
or sawed white oak ,walnut,locustor cedar timber.
§ 2. That no county bonds shallbe issuedto pay for
the building of turnpikes until one mile pike has been
completed, and then only for the part so completed
and received as hereinafter reviewed . There shall not
be any bond issuedfor lessthan one mile of turnpike
unless the fractionof a mile be the completion of same
line of road .
§ 3. The county judge,by and with the approval of
the board of justicesin sessionassembled, shallap.
point a man to inspect and receive the turnpikes
builded under this act ; said receiver shall execute
bond ,payable to Trimble county court, with approved
security,forthe faithfuldischargeof his duties; and
he shall receive as compensation for his services five
dollarsfor each mile of turnpike receivedby him , to
be paid out of fund raisedby taxation for turnpike
purposes; and ifhe should properly rejectany mile
of turnpike, he shallreceivethe same compensation,
to be paid by the builder or contractorof said turn-
pike .
§ 4. No turnpike shall be builded under this act,
and the act to which thisis an amendment , except
upon some important county road heretoforeestab-
lished, or upon a new road, with the concurrence of
the county court ; and all turnpikes now under con-
tractfor building shallbe builded as herein directed.
§ 5. The treasurerof allturnpike roads in Trimble,
in which the county has taken stock, shall, on the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 895
firstday of July and the firstday of December in each
year, report to the county court a fullstatement of
thetollscollectedand the amount expended for their
respectiveroads .
§ 6. This act to take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 18,1886.

CHAPTER 1295 .
AN ACT toprohibitthesaleof intoxicatingliquorsin Caldwellcounty,
and providing for a vote on same .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful within Caldwell coun-
ty tosell, or expose to sale, any intoxicatingliquors,
and whoever shalldo so, or shallkeep or have in said
countyany such liquor with intent to sellthe same ,
or with the intent that the same shall be sold in said
county, shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor ,
and, upon conviction, shall be fined three hundred
dollarsand imprisonedfiftydays in the county jail
foreach offense; and each selling,and each twenty-
four hours such liquor is exposed to sale in said
county,or kept with intentto sell,or that same may
be sold in said county, shall be deemed a separate
offense. Whoever shallsell,or exposetosale,insaid
county,or shall have or keep any such liquor with
any of the intents above-named , under pretense of
making an exhibitionor saleof any art, article, ani-
mal or thing whatsoever, or under pretenseof making

900 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
a gift,or of sellingor exhibitingsome other thing and
giving the liquor, or by the use of any trick, trade,
device, pretense, contrivance, apparatus, art, artifice,
game ,evasionor thing whatsoever,by whatever name
called, shallbe deemed guiltyunder thissection, and
be punished as above prescribed.
§ 2. That it shallbe unlawful to import, or cause
another to import,into Caldwell county, any intoxi-
cating liquor, with intent to violateor evade any of
the provisionsof this act; and whoever does so, or
whoever imports into said county for any other per-
son any such liquorwhen he knows ,or has reasonable
ground to believe,and does believe,thatsuch liquor
is intended to be used to violateor evade any of the
provisionsof thisact, shallbe deemed guiltyof a mis-
demeanor ,and ,upon conviction, shallbe punished as
prescribed in the firstsectionfor violationsof that
section; but nothing herein contained shall prevent
the importation intosaid county of any liquorwhich
isnot intended to be used in violatingor evading the
provisionsof this act. Under this sectionproof that
any one has possession, in said county, of intoxicat-
ing liquor shall be prima facie evidence that such
person imported saidliquorintosaid county ; but the
Commonwealth shallstillbe requiredto prove that it
was used, or was intendedto be used, to violateor
evade the provisions of this act.
§3 . Whoever shall haul,carry or transport, in any
way , any intoxicatingliquor in said county, or from
one placeor house in said county to another place or
house in said county, when he knows ,or when he has
reason to believeand does believe, that the article
which he is hauling,carrying or transportingis in-
toxicatingliquor, and that the same isintended to be
used in violatingin any way the provisions of this
act, shallbe deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and
shall, upon conviction, be fined three hundred dol-
larsand imprisoned fiftydays in the county jail: and
proof thathe did actuallyso haul,carry or transport

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 901
in any way any such liquor in said county,shall be
prima facie evidence that he knew at the time he
was so hauling, carrying or transporting the same
that it was intoxicatingliquor; but the Common-
wealth shall stillbe required to prove that such
liquor so hauled, carried or transportedwas used, or
intended to be used, in violatingor evading the pro-
visions of this act ; but nothing herein contained shall
prevent the bringingintosaid county any liquorwhich
isnot intended to be used in violatingor evading any
of the provisionsof thislaw .
4. That for the purposes of this act the words
"sell" and "sale," wherever they occur in this act,
shall be deemed and construed to mean and include
vend, sell, barter, trafficin, trade, exchange, loan,
procure for, and every dispositionwhich is, shall or
can be made of intoxicatingliquorin selling,or in the
attempt to sellthe same ,or in the attempt to violate
or evadetheprovisionsof thisact; and proofof the
factthat any person while in, on, or passing through
any house,room , tenement, tent,cave, car, boat, or
other structure,or premises adjacent thereto, in said
county, owned ,occupied or controlledby another, ob-
tained any intoxicatingliquor in any of the ways
named in section 1, or in any of the above-named
ways , or in any way , shallbe deemed prima facie
evidencethat the person who owns ,occupies or con-
trolssuch house , room , tenement , tent, cave , car, boat
or other structure,or premises adjacent thereto, sold
saidliquorso obtained. That for the purposes of this
act the word " import," in the second section, and
wherever it occurs in this act,shall be deemed to mean
and include brought, hauled, transported, carried,
sent and introduced into Caldwell county,and shall
apply to every person, whether he be the owner of the
liquoror not. That for the purposes of thisact the
words " intoxicatingliquor" and " liquor," wher-
ever they occur in thisact,shallbe construed to mean
whisky , brandy ,rum , gin,wine, ale,lager beer,bock

902 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
beer and any spirituous, vinous,malt or intoxicating
liquors, or any mixture, compound or combination
thereof, or any bitters,tonicsor drinks made there-
from,by whatever name called. That forthepurposes
of this act the word " whoever " and the word " per-
son," wherever they occur in thisact,shallbe deemed
and construed to mean and include person, persons,
society, club or combination of persons,partnership,
corporationor company , by whatever name called.
§5 . That nothing in this act shall be construed to
prohibitor prevent any railroadcompany or any com-
mon carrierfrom transporting, in good faith,any in-
toxicatingliquorthrough Caldwell county on itsway
to other counties, States or countries ; nor to prevent
the importation or sale of wine for sacramental pur-
poses .
§ 6. That the judge of the Caldwell quarterlycourt,
and the policecourt of any incorporatedtown in Cald-
well county, shallhave concurrentjurisdictionwith
the Caldwellcircuitcourt of allprosecutionsfor viola-
tions of the provisions of this act, and in the two
courtsfirstabove named ,no examining trialor indict-
ment shallbe had beforeproceeding to finaltrial, and
prosecutionsshallproceed by summons or warrant in
the name of the Commonwealth , as in any misde-
meanor case. All finesrecovered under this act shall,
except the portion thereofgoing to the officers, go to
the county of Caldwell,to assistin paying itsbonded
debt .
§ 7. This act shall become and be a law from its
passage,but none of itsprovisions(except those of the
sectionwhich ,by itsown terms, isto take effectfrom
and afterthe passage of thisact,without regard to
votetobe taken),shalltake effectuntilratifiedby a ma-
jorityof the legalvotersof Caldwellcounty,who shal
vote at an electionto be held on the Tuesday afterthe
firstMonday in November , 1886,thatisthe congres-
sional election day in said year , at which election all
the legal voters of said county who would on that day

be entitledto vote for a member of the Legislature, if
one were then to be voted for, shall be entitledto vote.
That whenever ten or more citizensof Caldwell county
shalldeposit with the county judge enough money to
pay the costs of the electionhereinprovided for, and
shall, in writing,requesthim to do so,the judge of
the Caldwell county courtshallmake an order on the
orderbook of said court directingthe sheriffof said

above-named ,shallact as officersof this election. It
shallbe the duty of the county court clerk of said
county to give to the sheriff, or other officerwhose
duty itisto hold said election,a certifiedcopy of the

CHAPTER 1296 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous, vinous and malt liquors
in Meade county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawfulfor any person or
persons to sell, barter or trafficin spirituous,vinous
or malt liquors,in any quantitywhatever,and for any
purpose whatever,eitherdirectlyor indirectly, in the
county of Meade .
§ 2. Any person violatingthe provisionsof the pre-
ceding sectionshall,upon conviction, be finedin any
sum not less than one hundred nor more than five
hundred dollars,in the discretionof the jury trying
the case .
§ 3. This act shall not take effector be in force until
the same shallbe ratifiedand approved by a majority
of the votes cast at the electionhereinafterprovided
for in this act ,
§ 4. Said electionshall be held at the regular elec-
tion held in November , 1886, and itshallbe unlawful
for any person to vote at said election who is not
eligibleto vote for Representative to the General
Assembly . It shallbe the duty of the clerk of the
Meade county court, in preparing the poll-books for
said election, to make one column marked at the head
thereof on each page, " For the sale of spirituous,
vinous and malt liquors," and anothercolumn marked

906 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
at the head of each page, " Against the saleof spirit-
uous, vinous and malt liquors."
§ 5. It shall be the duty of the clerk of said
electionto propound to each individual who offers
to vote at said electionthe question, "Are you for
or against the sale of spirituous, vinous and malt
liquors in Meade county ?" and his vote shall be
recorded according to his answer . The officersof
the election shall be governed by the laws now
regulatingthe conduct of officersof general elec-
tions. The poll-books shall be returned, compared
and result certifiedas now required by law regu-
latinggeneral elections. If it shall appear from a
comparison of the pollsthat a majority of the votes
cast at said electionare againstthe sale of said liq-
uors,then the board to examine the pollsshallmake
a certificateof that fact, which shall be filed with
the county clerk, who shall record the same in his
office, and then this act shall be in full force and
effect .
§ 6. It is hereby made the duty of the Meade
county court to order the sheriffto give twenty
days' notice of said election, by printed or written
posters,in each of the votingplacesof said county .
§ 7. Nothing in thisact shallbe construed to inter-
fere with the privilegeof any person who is selling
liquors under a licenseproperly granted tillthe ex-
pirationof such license.
§ 8. This act, in so far as it authorizes the vote to
be taken, shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved May 18, 1886 .

CHAPTER 1299 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof A. T. Curry, of Green county.
WHEREAS , Caroline Curry , of Green county ,Ken-
tucky, adjudged a pauper idiotin the Green circuit
court, fiveyears before the July term, 1885, of said
court, is yet alive, as appears from an order of said
court, made January 18, 1886, and was unable to
labor to support herself, and has no parents, and
no estate in possession, reversion or remainder :
and whereas, her committee, A. T. Curry , has cared
for and provided for said idiot sincethe eighteenth
day of July, 1885, to January 18,1886, as shown by
said order of said court, and was made an allowance
therefor by said court, but which amount was re-
fused payment by the Auditor , on account of an
oversightin said court in not having a new inquest,
as required by law ; now , therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and
he is hereby, directed to draw his warrant on the
Treasurer in favor of A. T. Curry for thirty-seven
dollars and fiftycents for keeping said idiot six
months , to be paid out of any money not otherwise
appropriated .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
[Became a law withoutthe approvalof the Governor,May 15,1886.]

CHAPTER 1302 .
AN ACT providingforthe assessmentand collectionof taxes on the
Providence Branch of the Louisville and Nashville Railroad Com-
pany .
WHEREAS ,It appears that the Madisonville and
Shawneetown Straight Line Railroad Company was
incorporatedby an act of this General Assembly ,
entitled"An act to incorporatethe Madisonville and
Shawneetown StraightLine Railroad Company ," ap
proved February 18, 1870, and that said company ,
afteritsorganization, partiallyconstructeda road -bed
and other works ,but failedto complete said road as
contemplated in saidactof incorporation; and where-
as, itappears from section40 of saidact that the same
shallbe exempt from taxation untilcompleted ; and
whereas, it appears that said corporation has long
sinceceased to exist,and that itssaid right of way ,
depot grounds,and other investments for the use of
saidroad,have become the property of the Louisville
and Nashville Railroad Company , and now known
and called the " Providence Branch " of said com-
pany . That in the year 1882 the said Louisvilleand
NashvilleRailroad Company completed said road to
Providence, a distance of seventeen miles, and has
failedto extend the same any further,and that said
company has failedand refusedto listor pay taxes on
said road on the ground that it is not completed ;
therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Louisville and Nashville Railroad
Company is hereby given the furthertime of two
years,from the passageof thisact,in which to com-
plete the constructionof the road mentioned in the
act,entitled"An act to incorporatethe Madisonville
and Shawneetown Straight Line Railroad Company ,"
approved February 18, 1870 ; and at the expirationof

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 913
said time the said road shall be deemed completed so
far as to render the same subject to taxation under
the laws of this State. That afterthe expirationof
said time the presidentand directorsof the Louis-
ville and NashvilleRailroad Company shallannually
list so much of said road as iscompleted and in oper-
ation in the same manner that other railroads are
listed, and the same shall be assessedand subject to
taxation for all purposes as other roads are taxed
under the laws of this State .
§ 2. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
[ Became a law withoutthe approvalof the Governor, May 15, 1886.]

CHAPTER 1306 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Newton Craig,of Scottcounty.
WHEREAS , Certain settlements have been made and
suits determined between the State and Newton Craig
growing out of the claims of the latter as agent of
the State and keeper of the penitentiary, through
commissioners appointed to settleand adjust the ac-
counts between them , and in said settlements, as is
allegedby Craig,certainmistakes were made against
him in givingtheStateimpropercreditsand in fail-
ing to allow him certainclaims to which he was en-
titled. Now ,in order to a finaladjustment of allmat-
ters between the State and said Craig , and to procure
a settlementupon the principlesof equityand justice,
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a commission consistingof three persons,
one of whom shallbe nained by the Governor , one by
Newton Craig, and the third by the two thus named ,
be,and the same ishereby,authorizedand empowered
to take intoconsiderationallof the reportsof former
commissioners, the late report of the Governor , and
all other documents connected with this matter, and
make a full and finalsettlement of this long and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 923
expensive matter. The Attorney-General is hereby
instructedto represent the State before thisboard of
commissioners, and Newton Craig may be represented
by counsel in all matters before said board of com-
missioners at his own expense ; and no person hold-
ing an officeunder the Stategovernment shall act as
an arbitrator or commissioner . All of said commis-
sioners herein authorized to be selected shall, before
actingas such, take an oath to act according to law
and evidence and the equity of the case to the best
of theirjudgment without favor or affection. When
their settlementisconcluded,and a majority of their
signaturesappended thereto,they shallcertifyto the
Auditor of Public Accounts the result,who is hereby
directed to draw his warrant on the Treasury for the
amount of theiraward , if any be found in favor of
said Craig .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
[Became a law withouttheapprovalof theGovernor,May 5,1886.]

CHAPTER 1310 .
AN ACT to protectfishand game in the countiesof Ballard and Car-
lisle.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful for any person , who is
not at the time a bona fide resident of the counties of
Ballard or Carlisle,in the Stateof Kentucky , to hunt
with dogs, guns or otherwise, or who shall kill by
shooting, trapping, or by any means , any game or
fowls good for food, or any animals whose skins or
fursare of value,within the limitsof said counties.
§ 2. That itshallbe unlawful for any person,who is
not at the time a bona fide resident of said counties
of Ballardor Carlisle, with hook, drag-net, dip-net, |
set-net,seine,or by any other means ,devices or con-
trivances,to catch,kill,destroyor take out of or from

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 939
the waters of any creek, river, lake, pond ,bayou or
body of water situatedwithin saidcounties.
§3 . Any person violatingeither of the foregoing
sections of this act shall be fined in a sum not ex-
ceeding thirtydollars for each offense, to be recov-
erable before any justiceof the peace of said coun-
ties upon warrant or upon an indictmentin the cir
cuit court thereof .
§ 4. This act shall take effect from and after the
firstday of June , 1886 .
[Became a law withoutthe approva of theGovernor,April30,1886.]

CHAPTER 1312 .

ander Lowe ,of Boyd county,Kentucky,under legal

saidLowe ,afterobtainingsaidreceipt,soldthereunder
some malt liquor, for which he was at once prose

CHAPTER 1313 .

pany .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly ofthe Com
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. C. Holt, R. W. Holland, Jesse Col-
bert, Joshua Woodhead , C. H. Lee, Sr., and J.K.
Wandelohr , be, and they are hereby, constituteda
body -corporate and politic, under the name of the
Pendleton Natural Gas and Fuel Company , with
perpetual succession; and as such shall have the
right to sue and be sued, plead and be impleaded,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 941
in all the courts of this Commonwealth ; to acquire,
hold , dispose of real and personal estate, eitherby
lienor purchase ; to contractand be contractedwith ;
to have a common seal and to alterat pleasure; to
make and enforce by -laws, rules and regulationsfor
the conduct of its affairs, and to alter and amend
the same at pleasure; to contractwith any person or
corporation,eitherin or out of thisState,or to con-
solidatewith same ; and for that purpose such person
or persons, corporationor corporations, are hereby
empowered to contractor consolidatewith said com-
pany , upon such terms as may be mutually agreed
upon ; to issuebonds ,securingthe same by mortgage
to a trustee of all its properties, rights and fran-
chises, including its corporate privilegesand name ,
not exceeding the sum of two hundred thousand
dollars .
§ 2. The object and purpose of this corporation
shall be to furnish to the citizensand corporations
of this and other States natural gas ; and for the
purpose of so doing the capitalstock of same shallbe
two hundred thousand dollars,divided into two thou-
sand shares of one hundred dollars each ; and the
same may be increasedat pleasureby the board of di-
rectors. For the purpose of erectingsuitableworks ,
conducting itsbusiness, laying pipes,etc., said com-
pany shall have the right of eminent domain to be
exercised by proceedings similar to those provided
by chapter 94 of the General Statutes, and to these
ends fullpower is hereby conferred upon the sev-
eral county courts of this State: And provided fur-
ther, That if it shallrefuseto furnish and supply to
any cityor town , or to the citizensthereof,said gas,
where the same can be so furnished , then such city
or town ,or the citizensthereof, may do so themselves:
And provided further, That out of the net income
from said business, afterthe payment to the stock-
holdersof a dividendof six per cent. per annum ,
there shall be paid into the State Treasury, for the

942 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
benefitof the common schoolfund, a sum equal tosix
per cent . of the remainder for each year .

right to open books of subscriptionto the capital
stock of said company , and when shallhave
been subscribed, may organize said company by the
electionof fivedirectors,who shallbe electedannually

January . Said board of directorsshallelectfrom its
members a president,vice-presidentand treasurer,and
may elector appoint from the stockholderssuch other
officersas may be provided for in the by -laws of said
company ; and the number of directorsmay be in-
creased by the board at any time to not exceeding
nine .
§4 . Said company shall annually furnish to the
Auditor of State a sworn statement of its net earnings .
§5 . The privateproperty of the stockholdersshall
be exempt from liabilityforcorporatedebts.
§ 6. The principal place of business of said company
shall be in the town of Falmouth ; but it may have
such branch officesas the board of directors may de-
termine .

CHAPTER 1323 .
AN ACT for the benefitof S. H. Wells, clerk of the county court of
Todd county .
WHEREAS , S. H. Wells , clerk of the county court
of Todd county,shortlyafterthe passage of the act
of 1883-4 , increasing the tax on license to merchants ,
etc., to sellspirituousliquors,did,early in the spring
of 1885, through honest mistake and misconstruction
of saidact,and by and under the advice of the coun-
ty judge of Todd county,issue fivedifferen'tlicenses
to merchants to sellspirituousliquorsunder saidact,
and collectedthereforfiftydollarsfor each license,
when , as he afterwards ascertained, he should have
collectedone hundred dollars for each of said license;
and whereas,said clerk has been unable,sincehe as-
certainedsaid fact,to collectthe remaining fiftydol-
larson each of said license; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That S. H. Wells ,clerk of the county court of
Todd county,be,and he is hereby ,releasedfrom any
and all responsibilityhe is or may be under to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , by reason of his failure
to collectone hundred dollars instead of fiftydollars
for each of said merchant's license so issued .
§ 2. This act shall be in force from and after its
passage .
[Became a law withouttheapprovaloftheGovernor,May 15,1886.]
